products.title: Ancient Wood
products.body_html:
Ancient Wood

Ancient Wood is a carbonized driftwood highly valued in aquascaping for its distinct natural beauty and versatility in crafting breathtaking underwater landscapes.

Renowned for its durability, Ancient Wood remains intact even after prolonged submersion, making it a long-lasting addition to your aquarium. While mostly inert, it may release tannins initially, causing a slight reduction in pH levels. This effect is usually minimal but should be monitored if maintaining specific water parameters is critical.

Preparation Tips:

  • Soaking: Submerge Ancient Wood in water for several days before use to help release residual tannins and ensure it sinks properly.
  • Cleaning: Thoroughly clean the wood to remove any dust or debris before adding it to your aquarium.
  • Placement: Arrange the wood thoughtfully to enhance the natural aesthetic, ensuring it complements plants, substrates, and other decorative elements.

Ancient Wood is an excellent choice for aquascapers looking to bring a touch of natural elegance to their aquascapes.

products.title: Aqua Natural Flamingo Gravel Substrate
products.body_html:
Flamingo Gravel

Aqua Natural Flamingo Gravel Substrate adds a vibrant touch to any freshwater aquarium with its unique pink hue. Made from natural river gravel, it’s safe, non-toxic, and free from harmful chemicals or dyes. This eco-friendly substrate supports healthy aquascapes by providing a stable base for plants and decorations while promoting water flow and biological filtration. Ideal for planted tanks, community setups, and dark water environments, Flamingo Gravel enhances your aquarium's aesthetic with its unique color and texture. Easy to maintain and long-lasting, it's the perfect choice for creating a beautiful, natural underwater landscape.

Using Your Flamingo Gravel:

  • Prepare the Gravel: Rinse the gravel thoroughly with clean water to remove any dust or fine particles before adding it to your aquarium.
  • Layer the Substrate: Spread the gravel evenly across the bottom. For planted tanks, layer it to a depth of about 2-4 inches (5-10 cm) to support root growth.
  • Add Plants and Decorations: Place live plants, rocks, driftwood, and other decorations on top of the gravel, securing them in place with the gravel’s weight.
  • Fill the Aquarium with Water: Fill the tank slowly, placing a plate or plastic bag on top of the gravel to minimize disruption.
  • Regular Maintenance: Regularly vacuum the substrate during water changes to remove waste and maintain water quality. This will help prevent harmful buildup and keep your tank in optimal condition.

Key Characteristics:

1. Natural Composition

  • Natural River Gravel: Flamingo Aquarium Gravel is sourced from natural river gravel, ensuring a safe and non-toxic environment for your aquatic life. It’s free from harmful chemicals, dyes, and artificial coatings, making it an ideal choice for fish, invertebrates, and plants.
  • Environmentally Friendly: This gravel is carefully harvested and processed to maintain its natural integrity, offering a sustainable substrate option for aquascaping.

2. Unique Appearance

  • Vibrant Flamingo Pink Color: The gravel’s striking flamingo pink hue brings a vibrant splash of color to any aquarium. This natural color remains intact over time, enhancing your aquascaping without fading.
  • Varied Pebble Sizes: Composed of small, rounded pebbles that vary slightly in size, the gravel’s texture adds depth and a more organic appearance to your aquarium floor.

3. Aquarium Suitability

  • Freshwater Aquariums: Perfect for freshwater setups, Flamingo Aquarium Gravel is suitable for community tanks, planted aquariums, dark water tanks, and various species of freshwater fish.
  • Planted Tanks: This gravel’s natural composition supports root-feeding plants by providing stability for roots and promoting water flow to avoid anaerobic zones.
  • Dark Water Tanks: The gravel’s pink tones complement tannin-rich water, adding depth and natural variation for an enhanced look.

4. Functional Benefits

  • Stable Substrate for Aquascaping: The gravel’s varying size and weight make it ideal for anchoring decorations, plants, and other aquascaping elements.
  • Encourages Natural Behavior: It creates a natural habitat for bottom-dwelling species, promoting behaviors such as digging and foraging.
  • Supports Biological Filtration: The gravel’s surface area fosters the growth of beneficial bacteria, aiding in biological filtration to break down waste and maintain healthy water.
  • Inert Substrate: Since it is chemically inert, Flamingo Aquarium Gravel doesn’t alter water pH or hardness, making it compatible with a wide range of freshwater species.

5. Easy Maintenance

  • Low Dust: The gravel is low in dust, making it easy to rinse and clean, preventing water clouding when setting up the aquarium.
  • Long-Lasting: Its durable natural composition ensures the gravel remains intact over time, providing a long-lasting, low-maintenance substrate for your aquarium.

products.title: Aqua Natural Gold Pearl Bio-Substrate
products.body_html:
Gold Pearl Bio-Substrate

Aqua Natural Gold Pearl Bio-Substrate is a premium substrate designed to enhance both the beauty and health of your aquatic environment. Featuring a warm golden hue, this substrate provides a natural aesthetic that complements any freshwater aquarium or aquascape. Its natural composition not only enhances the visual appeal but also supports the biological balance of your tank, creating an ideal foundation for a thriving aquatic ecosystem.

Gold Pearl Bio-Substrate is carefully crafted from high-quality, non-toxic materials, making it safe for fish, invertebrates, and plants. Its porous texture promotes beneficial bacteria growth, helping to maintain optimal water quality and stability. This substrate is pre-washed to reduce dust and cloudiness, ensuring a hassle-free setup. Perfect for planted tanks, it offers a stable base for plant roots, encouraging healthy and vibrant growth. Aqua Natural Gold Pearl Bio-Substrate combines functionality with class, making it a must-have for any aquarist seeking natural underwater habitat.

Nitrifying Bacteria

Each pack includes bio-activated live nitrifying bacteria, designed to be used as a standalone substrate or mixed with dry substrate to promote a balanced and healthy aquarium. The patented formula contains live Nitrosomonas and Nitrobacter bacteria, which efficiently break down harmful ammonia and convert it into nitrates, supporting water quality.

Directions for Use:

Primary Use (Without Mixing with Dry Substrate):

  • Use 1 lb (450 g) of Bio-Substrate for every 1 gallon (3.8 liters) of water.
  • Do not rinse the substrate before use.
  • Spread it evenly and slowly fill the tank with water to avoid disturbing the substrate.
  • Start the filter after the tank is filled. Any initial cloudiness will settle over time.

Mixed Use (Combined with Dry Substrate):

  • Use 1 lb (450 g) of mixed live Bio-Substrate and dry substrate per 1 gallon (3.8 liters) of water at a 1:4 ratio (1 part live to 4 parts dry).
  • Do not rinse the substrate before use.
  • Thoroughly mix the Bio-Substrate with the dry substrate, spread evenly, and fill the tank slowly to minimize disturbance.
  • Start the filter once the tank is filled. Initial cloudiness will clear over time.

The live Nitrosomonas and Nitrobacter bacteria in this product are completely safe for aquatic life. For best results, pair with Aqua Natural’s dry substrate options.

Offer this food two to three times weekly as part of a varied diet. Provide only what your fish can consume within a few minutes.

Directions for Use

Shake a desired amount directly into the aquarium. Secure the lid promptly after use. Avoid touching or inhaling dust particles. Wash hands thoroughly with soap and water if contact occurs, and avoid rubbing eyes.

Important Notes

  • Not intended for human consumption.
  • Keep out of reach of children.

Allergy Warning

Dust from this product may cause allergic reactions in sensitive individuals. If symptoms arise, discontinue use immediately and seek medical advice. Avoid use if you have asthma, hay fever, or known allergies to insect larvae or crustaceans. Frequent users should take precautions to avoid contact or inhalation. Sensitization may occur with repeated exposure.

Disposal Instructions

Dispose of the container responsibly after use and do not reuse it.

products.title: Aqua Natural Bloodworms
products.body_html:
Bloodworms

Bloodworms are an excellent choice for small to medium-sized freshwater and marine fish, offering a nutrient-rich blend packed with essential amino acids, fats, and proteins to support their overall health and vitality. The advanced freeze-drying process ensures the natural flavor and nutrients of live bloodworms are retained, while guaranteeing the product is free from parasites and harmful diseases, making it a safe and nutritious option for your aquatic pets.

Ingredients

100% Bloodworms (Glycera)

Guaranteed Analysis

  • Crude Protein: Minimum 56.0%
  • Crude Fat: Minimum 3.0%
  • Crude Fiber: Maximum 5.0%
  • Moisture: Maximum 5.0%

Feeding Instructions

Feed this product two to three times weekly alongside other types of fish food. Offer only as much as your fish can consume within a few minutes.

Directions for Use

Dispense the desired amount directly into your aquarium. Close the container tightly after use. Avoid direct contact and inhalation of dust. Wash hands with soap and water if contact occurs, and refrain from rubbing your eyes.

Important Information

  • Not intended for human consumption.
  • Keep out of reach of children.

Allergy Warning

Dust from this product may cause allergic reactions in sensitive individuals. If symptoms occur, stop using immediately and consult a healthcare provider. Avoid use if you have asthma, hay fever, or known allergies to insect larvae or crustaceans. Regular users should minimize contact and avoid inhaling dust, as prolonged exposure may lead to sensitization.

Disposal Instructions

Dispose of the container responsibly after use. Do not reuse.

products.title: Congo Wood
products.body_html:
Congo Wood

Congo Wood is a more dense type of wood that we offer. It significantly contributes to the overall health of your aquarium ecosystem and livestock. As Congo Wood gradually releases tannins, it can slightly lower pH levels and create a more natural, blackwater environment that many fish species prefer. Congo Wood also supports beneficial bacteria growth, which is essential for maintaining water quality. With beneficial bacteria growth, congo wood will also produce biofilm which many aquatic pets will feed on such as shrimp and snails. With Congo Wood, you're not just adding décor to your tank, but also fostering a healthier, more dynamic ecosystem for your aquatic life.

Key Features:

  • Essential for Shrimp Breeding 
  • Creates Surface Area for Bio-film and Bacterial to Grow
  • Releases Beneficial Tannin into the Water which Simulates the Natural Habitat of Shrimp
  • Provides Hiding Places for Shrimp
  • Natural Feeding Ground for Shrimp of all Types

products.title: Blue Steel Shrimp
products.body_html:
Blue Steel Shrimp

Blue Steel Shrimp are a type of Caridina that are renowned for their striking blue coloration and unique steel-like sheen. Blue Steel Shrimp bring a touch of elegance to any freshwater aquarium, with their vibrant hues standing out beautifully against plants and decorations. Since Blue Steel Shrimp are a Caridina, they are slightly more sensitive to water conditions, making them a rewarding choice for shrimp keepers who enjoy a bit of a challenge. Blue Steel Shrimp thrive in well-maintained tanks with stable water parameters and make great companions for peaceful fish and other shrimp species.

Blue Steel Shrimp are natural algae eaters, helping to keep your aquarium clean and balanced. Their active and peaceful nature adds life to your tank without causing any disruption to other inhabitants. As Blue Steel Shrimp forage and explore their environment, they contribute to the overall health and vibrancy of your aquatic ecosystem. 

Tank Parameters

  • pH: 5.5-6.5
  • GH: 4-6
  • KH:  0-1
  • TDS: 120 to 160
  • Temp: 68 - 72°F

Tank Equipment

  • Filter: Matten Filter
  • Active Substrate: Brightwell Rio Escuro or Cafe Soil, Fluval Shrimp Stratum
  • Water: RO/DI Water remineralized with Shrimple GH

Please Note: All fish shipments have a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee when shipping with Overnight (1 Day) option. All other options void our Live Arrival Guarantee for fish.

products.title: Mix + Match Crypt Plant Bundle
products.body_html:
Crypt Bundle

Add some new plants to your aquarium with our Mix + Match Crypt Plant Bundle! Simply pick the Crypts of your choice to create your bundle and save!

Choose from:

  • Crypt Wendtii Red - A stunning aquarium plant known for its vibrant, reddish-brown leaves that add warmth and color to any aquascape.
  • Crypt Parva - a compact, slow-growing aquarium plant with lush green leaves, ideal for adding dense foreground coverage and a natural aesthetic to any aquascape.
  • Crypt Spiralis - A striking aquarium plant with long, slender, dark green leaves that create a beautiful, flowing effect, adding depth and a natural look to any aquascape.
  • Crypt Lutea - A hardy, low-maintenance plant that adds natural beauty and depth to freshwater aquariums with deep green to brownish leaves.
  • Crypt Undulata - A hardy, low-maintenance aquatic plant with wavy, vibrant green leaves that add a lush, natural look to any aquarium.
  • Crypt Balansae - A striking aquarium plant known for its long, narrow leaves that create a lush, elegant look, making it a perfect addition to aquascapes seeking a more natural, forest-like aesthetic.

products.title: Mini Tiger Endler
products.body_html:
Mini Tiger Endler

The Mini Tiger Endler is a vibrant and energetic addition to any freshwater aquarium. Known for its stunning tiger-like stripes and bright yellow coloration, the Mini Tiger Endler is a small but lively fish that brings a splash of color to your tank. Their active swimming patterns and friendly nature make them an ideal choice for both beginners and experienced aquarists. They thrive in well-planted tanks and enjoy the company of other peaceful fish, adding a dynamic yet harmonious presence to your aquarium ecosystem.

The Mini Tiger Endler is also incredibly easy to care for, making it perfect for hobbyists looking for low-maintenance, colorful additions. The Mini Tiger Endler is adaptable to a range of water conditions and requires minimal feeding. Their playful behavior and striking appearance not only enhance the aesthetic appeal of your tank but also create a captivating visual experience. Add them to your aquarium for a boost of color and life!

What we feed Endlers

We feed our fish a mix of frozen, live and prepared foods including live baby brine and black worms. We also supplement their diet with a mix of Northfin Veggie, Community and Bug Pro. 

Our Tank Parameters for Endlers

  • Ph: 6.5 to 7.8
  • Gh: 6 to 18
  • Kh:  2 to 6
  • TDS: 100 to 200
  • Temp: 72 to 80

products.title: 3 Tiger Nerite Snails
products.body_html:
Pack of 3 Tiger Nerite Snails

Tiger Nerite Snails are a fantastic addition to any freshwater aquarium, known for their beautiful, striped shells and excellent algae-cleaning abilities. Tiger nerite snails have a striking tiger-like pattern and effortlessly bring both functionality and beauty to aquariums. Tiger nerite snails remain small, growing up to 1 inch, making them suitable for tanks of various sizes, from nano aquariums to larger setups. Tiger Nerites are peaceful creatures that won’t disturb plants or other tank inhabitants, making them ideal for community aquariums.

In addition to their eye-catching appearance, Tiger Nerites are renowned for their ability to keep algae under control, including stubborn types like green spot algae. Tiger Nerites are easy to care for, thriving in well-maintained freshwater environments with stable water parameters. These snails cannot breed in freshwater, so you won’t have to worry about overpopulation. With their low maintenance needs and efficient cleaning skills, Tiger Nerite Snails are perfect for aquarists looking to maintain a healthy, vibrant tank while adding a unique aesthetic touch.

Care Guide for Tiger Nerite Snail

  • Diet: Primarily scavengers; High-quality foods like Kats Aquatics Calcium foods, NorthFin Veggie
  • Social Behavior: Peaceful
  • Native Habitat: Philippines
  • Average Adult Size: 1.2 inches (3 cm)
  • Typical Purchase Size: 0.3 - 1 inch (1.3 – 2.5 cm)

To maintain the health of your Tiger Nerite Snail, ensure an alkaline environment, and consider calcium supplementation, especially when using R/O water, to support the integrity of their stunning shells. 

Please note: During shipping, snails sometimes go into a hibernation-like state and may not become fully active for up to a week once acclimated to your aquarium. 
 

Tank Parameters for Tiger Nerite Snails

  • pH: 7.2 to 8.5
  • Gh: 7 to 15
  • Kh:  2 to 8
  • TDS: 120 to 400
  • Temp: 68 to 78

Tank Equipment for Tiger Nerite Snails

  • Filter: Matten Filter
  • Substrate: Pool Filter Sand
  • Water: RO/DI Water remineralized with Shrimple Gh/Kh+
  • Decor: Malaysian Driftwood, Seiryu Stone, Elephant Skin Stone

products.title: Rainbow Goby | Stiphodon ornatus
products.body_html:
Rainbow Goby (Stiphodon ornatus)

The Rainbow Goby (Stiphodon ornatus), also called the Rainbow Stiphodon Goby or Rainbow Goby Fish, is a small and colorful fish that's perfect for any freshwater aquarium. Males are known for their bright colors, showing off shades of blue, orange, and red. They are peaceful fish that get along well with shrimp and other calm fish, making them a great choice for community tanks.

Rainbow Gobies are known for eating algae, which helps keep the tank clean. They do best in tanks with moving water and a sandy or rocky bottom, similar to the streams they come from. They only grow to about 2-3 inches, so they can live in smaller tanks as long as the water is clean and well-filtered.

Please Note: All fish shipments have a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee when shipping with Overnight (1 Day) option. All other options void our Live Arrival Guarantee for fish. 

What size tank do Rainbow Gobies need?

Rainbow Gobies are small fish, growing to about 2-3 inches, so they can live in tanks as small as 10 gallons. However, they thrive best in tanks with at least 20 gallons of water, especially when kept in groups or with other species​.

What should I feed my Rainbow Goby?

Rainbow Gobies are primarily algae eaters. They love grazing on biofilm and algae in the tank, but you should also provide them with supplemental food like algae wafers, blanched vegetables, and occasionally high-quality sinking pellets​. We supplement their diet with a mix of fresh vegetables and Northfin Veggie, Community, Bug Pro, and Northfin Kelp Wafers to ensure a balanced and nutritious diet. We also feed our Gobys prepared algae wafers and let them graze on natural algae and bio-film. This can be supplemented by using Bacter AE.

Can Rainbow Gobies live with shrimp and other fish?

Yes! Rainbow Gobies are peaceful fish and are shrimp-safe, meaning they can coexist with adult shrimp without harming them. They also do well in community tanks with other non-aggressive fish​.

What water conditions do Rainbow Gobies need?

Rainbow Gobies need clean, well-filtered water with stable conditions. They prefer slightly alkaline water with a pH of 7.0 to 8.0 and a temperature range of 72-82°F. Since they come from fast-flowing streams, they also do well in tanks with moderate water flow​.

Do Rainbow Gobies need a specific type of substrate?

Yes, they prefer tanks with smooth sand or small pebbles that mimic their natural riverbed habitat. This type of substrate allows them to graze on algae and biofilm, their primary food source​.

How many Rainbow Gobies should I keep together?

You can keep a single Rainbow Goby, but they are more active and social in groups. It's recommended to keep at least 3-4 together if space allows. Males can be territorial, so providing enough space and hiding spots is essential​.

Do Rainbow Gobies breed in home aquariums?

Breeding Rainbow Gobies in home aquariums is rare. In the wild, they lay eggs in streams, and their larvae are swept into the ocean before returning to freshwater as juveniles. Replicating this process in a tank environment is quite challenging​.

Our Tank Parameters for Rainbow Gobies

To ensure your Rainbow Goby (Stiphodon ornatus) thrives, we maintain specific water parameters that mimic their natural environment. Here’s a breakdown of the ideal conditions we use in our tanks:

  • pH: 7.0 to 8.0 – Rainbow Gobies prefer slightly alkaline water, which supports their health and well-being.
  • GH (General Hardness): 4 to 10 – This range offers the right balance of minerals like calcium and magnesium, keeping their bones and overall health in check.
  • KH (Carbonate Hardness): 2 to 6 – Adequate KH helps stabilize the pH levels, preventing drastic fluctuations that could stress the fish.
  • TDS (Total Dissolved Solids): 100 to 150 – A lower TDS keeps the water clean and close to the conditions these fish experience in nature.
  • Temperature: 70 to 74°F – Maintaining this cool, moderate temperature range helps the Rainbow Goby remain comfortable and active.

By keeping your tank within these parameters, you can create an ideal habitat that allows Rainbow Gobies to thrive.

products.title: Cinnamon Bark - 40g
products.body_html:
Cinnamon Bark for Shrimp Aquarium

Shrimp Aquarium Cinnamon Bark increases the vitality and wellbeing of your aquarium inhabitants in a purely natural way. Valuable tannins, various essential oils, and humic substances contribute to good water quality and protect and care for your shrimps, crayfish, and other aquarium inhabitants.

What are the benefits of Shrimp Aquarium Cinnamon Bark?

The dried cinnamon bark is not only used by shrimps, crayfish, crabs, snails, and fish (e.g. sucking catfish) as a shelter, hiding and spawning place, but also as a valuable source of dietary fiber. This makes cinnamon bark an excellent addition to your aquascape to benefit aquatic lifeforms, but also because of its unique reddish coloring.

Why Shrimp Aquarium Cinnamon Bark?

The Shrimp Aquarium Cinnamon Bark is made of real Ceylon cinnamon, which is free of coumarin. Cassia cinnamon (Cinnamomum cassia), which is often sold by aquarium hobbyists and is often available in craft shops, contains very harmful doses of the substance coumarin. Coumarin is suspected of causing liver and kidney damage as well as cancer in high doses.

How to use Shrimp Aquarium Cinnamon Bark?

We recommend adding about 5 sticks of cinnamon bark per 15 gallons of water in your tank, as the cinnamon may lower the pH of your water slightly. While this does have its beneficial effects, lowering the pH too far may negatively impact the wellbeing of your fish.

products.title: Wrestling Halfbeak
products.body_html:
Wrestling Halfbeak Livebearer

Wrestling Halfbeak is a fascinating surface-dweller that will add excitement to your aquarium. Found in various aquatic habitats such as canals, drains, flooded fields, and more, this elongate fish thrives in sluggish, heavily vegetated waters.

To create an ideal habitat for the Wrestling Halfbeak, it is recommended to set up the aquarium in a quiet area with a large surface area. This species can be skittish, so providing ample space is crucial to prevent any damage to its long, delicate mouth parts. A combination of tall driftwood tangles, dense planting, and floating species will provide a sense of security for these fish.

Efficient filtration and gentle water movement are important considerations for the Wrestling Halfbeak's well-being. Regular small water changes will help maintain optimal water quality by minimizing nitrate levels. It is advisable to keep these fish in groups, either with a single male and multiple females or, in larger setups with visual barriers, mixed groups consisting of at least 6 males. This helps distribute any territorial aggression among the males.

  • Gh: 8 to 16

products.title: Striped Rubbernose Pleco | Chaetostoma Thomasi L187b
products.body_html:
Striped Rubbernose Pleco

Striped Rubbernose Pleco id a distinctive and eye-catching addition to your aquarium that showcases a striking striped pattern and unique appearance. With its engaging features and fascinating behavior, this pleco species promises to captivate enthusiasts and become a standout feature in any aquatic environment.

Key Features:

  1. Bold Striped Pattern: The Striped Rubbernose Pleco boasts a captivating striped pattern that runs along its body, adding a dynamic and visually appealing element to your aquarium. The contrast between the dark stripes and lighter background creates a stunning display that is sure to catch the eye.

  2. Unique Rubbernose: Characterized by its distinctive rubbery snout, this pleco species has a unique appearance that sets it apart from other aquatic inhabitants. Its specialized snout is adapted for scraping algae and detritus, making it an excellent addition for maintaining a clean and healthy aquarium.

  3. Peaceful Nature: Known for their peaceful demeanor, Striped Rubbernose Plecos coexist harmoniously with a variety of tankmates. Create a serene and balanced aquatic environment as these gentle plecos interact peacefully with other community fish.

  4. Algae-Eating Behavior: As voracious algae-eaters, Striped Rubbernose Plecos play a vital role in maintaining the cleanliness of your aquarium. Their constant grazing helps control algae growth, ensuring a pristine and well-maintained aquatic ecosystem.

  5. Low-Maintenance Care: Suited for aquarists of all levels, the Striped Rubbernose Pleco is relatively easy to care for. With a well-maintained aquarium and a balanced diet, these plecos thrive and add a unique charm to your underwater realm.

  6. Dietary Needs: Offer a varied diet to ensure the optimal health and well-being of your Striped Rubbernose Pleco. Provide sinking algae wafers, fresh vegetables such as zucchini or cucumber, and occasional protein-rich foods like bloodworms or brine shrimp to meet their dietary requirements.

products.title: Scissortail Rasbora
products.body_html:
Introducing the Scissortail Rasbora: Unveil the Beauty of Nature in Your Aquarium!

Discover the exquisite beauty of the Scissortail Rasbora, a mesmerizing freshwater fish species that will breathe life into your aquarium like never before. With its stunning appearance and peaceful nature, this graceful creature is the perfect addition to any aquatic enthusiast's collection.

Characterized by its vibrant coloration and unique scissor-shaped tail, the Scissortail Rasbora (Rasbora trilineata) is native to the lush waters of Southeast Asia. Its body shimmers with a radiant iridescence, showcasing hues of silver and gold that reflect the light in a captivating dance.

Ideal for both novice and experienced aquarists, the Scissortail Rasbora is known for its social and peaceful temperament, making it an excellent choice for community tanks. Their friendly and non-aggressive nature allows them to thrive in harmony alongside other compatible fish species, creating a visually stunning and harmonious aquatic ecosystem.

With a preference for well-maintained aquariums, these Rasboras will flourish in environments that mimic their natural habitats. We recommend providing them with ample swimming space, dense vegetation, and smooth, rounded gravel to mimic riverbeds. Additionally, ensuring a well-balanced diet of high-quality flakes, pellets, and occasional live or frozen foods will contribute to their overall health and vibrant coloration.

As an added bonus, observing the Scissortail Rasbora's schooling behavior is a true delight. Watch as they gracefully glide through the water, moving in synchronized harmony with their companions, exhibiting a captivating spectacle that's bound to enchant both young and old alike.

Key Features:

  • Stunning and vibrant coloration with iridescent silver and gold tones
  • Graceful scissor-shaped tail adds an alluring touch to your aquarium
  • Peaceful and friendly temperament, perfect for community tanks
  • Schooling behavior creates a mesmerizing display of synchronized movement
  • Thrives in well-maintained aquariums with ample swimming space and vegetation

Embrace the wonder of the aquatic world with the Scissortail Rasbora. Bring home this enchanting beauty, and witness how it transforms your aquarium into a lively and picturesque underwater paradise. Explore the allure of nature with these captivating fish, and let them become the centerpiece of your aquatic realm.

products.title: Silver Kuhli Loach
products.body_html:
Silver Kuhli Loach

The Silver Kuhli Loach is a super entertaining fish that adds both beauty and intrigue to your freshwater aquarium. The Silver Kuhli Loach has a sleek, eel-like body and shimmers silver tones. The Silver Kuhli Loach is known for its peaceful nature and is a social species that thrives in groups. The Silver Kuhli Loach is often seen burrowing in the substrate or exploring crevices. They are most active during twilight hours, making them a unique addition to your tank's dynamic.

Beyond their striking appearance and entertaining nature, Silver Kuhli Loaches are excellent at helping to keep the substrate clean, as they sift through the sand in search of food. They are compatible with a wide range of peaceful tank mates, making them a versatile choice for community aquariums. With proper care and a well-maintained environment, these loaches can live for several years, providing endless fascination as they display their natural behaviors in your aquatic setup.

What are Silver Kuhli Loaches like?

The Silver Kuhli Loach is a real night owl. They are active mainly at night and can peacefully co-exist with most other fish. During the night, they will look for food that sunk to the bottom of the aquarium. On top of that, their natural curiosity will drive them to explore any crevices and caves that might be around in their habitat.

The ideal conditions for the Silver Kuhli

Despite the fact that SIlver Kuhli Loaches are scavengers, they still require a relatively clean, well-oxygenated tank. Silver Kuhli Loaches prefer a soft substrate, as it makes searching for food more manageable, so sand or fine gravel would be best. The water should be soft, slightly acidic and at a temperature of 73 – 86 °F, as these are tropical fish. You should also make sure that there is moderate lighting in their habitat.

Our Tank Parameters:

  • Ph: 6.5 to 7.1
  • Gh: 8 to 12
  • Kh:  2 to 4
  • Temp: 72 to 80

    Tank Recommendations:

    • Rock: Smooth rock (round in shape)
    • Leaves: Indian Almond or Guava
    • Cones: Alder, Giant Alder, Cyprus or Casuarina

products.title: Cryptocoryne Balansae | Balansae Crypt
products.body_html:
Cryptocoryne Balansae

Cryptocoryne Balansae is a visually stunning aquatic plant, known for its long, slender, and wavy-edged leaves that provide an elegant, natural look to your aquarium. As it can reach heights of up to 28 inches, it’s ideal for background placement in larger aquariums, creating a lush, flowing effect that enhances the overall aesthetics of your tank. This versatile plant thrives in a variety of conditions and adapts well to low to medium lighting, making it suitable for both beginner and experienced aquarists. Although CO2 supplementation isn't necessary, it can help improve its growth rate and vibrancy.

In addition to its aesthetic appeal, Cryptocoryne Balansae is known for being hardy and low maintenance. It tolerates a range of water parameters, including softer, more acidic water and slightly harder conditions. The plant’s crinkled leaves not only provide a beautiful texture to the aquascape but also serve as a great hiding spot for small fish and shrimp. Its ability to thrive without constant attention makes it a perfect addition for those looking to enhance the natural beauty of their aquarium with minimal effort.

Caring for Balansae Crypt

Scientific Name: Cryptocoryne crispatula var. balansae
Care Level: Easy
Lighting Requirement: Low to Medium
Growth Rate: Slow to Moderate
Max Size: 16-28 inches
CO2 Requirement: Not required, but CO2 supplementation can boost growth

 *All Plants may contain snails or snail eggs. We keep them in all of our tanks for the benefits they provide.*

Add a bright pop of color and activity to freshwater tanks with the Lambchop Rasbora, Trigonostigma espei! As their fun nickname suggests, these little fish exhibit an elegant shape highlighted by a vibrant reddish patch resembling a lamb chop.

Ranging 1.5-2 inches at maturity, Lambchops are ideal for nano community aquariums. Their small stature allows them to carefully maneuver and display schooling behavior. Groups of 6 or more really allow them to exhibit stunning synchronized movements set against lush plantscapes. Despite small sizes, their red coloration makes them stand out.

products.title: Lemon Tetra | Hyphessobrycon Pulchripinnis
products.body_html:
Lemon Tetra Fish

The Lemon Tetra or Hyphessobrycon Pulchripinnis is an inquisitive freshwater fish, lovingly named after its vibrant yellow coloring and round bodies often likened to a small lemon. Lemon Tetra is ideal for living in community aquariums and are happiest amongst similarly-sized tankmates. These vibrant fish prefer to live in mixed-sex groups of eight or more.


What Are Lemon Tetra Like?


Lemon Tetra are peaceful tropical fish but are certainly enjoyable to watch as they are
active swimmers throughout the day, making them a popular choice for aquarists. Growing to around two inches in length, they are ideal for smaller aquariums and can be paired with other friendly tetras.


Who Is Suited To Tetra?


If you’re looking for an adaptable and friendly tropical fish, a Lemon Tetra is a fantastic
choice. They are ideal for beginner to intermediate owners thanks to their peaceful
demeanor, easy care requirements, and can live up to eight years with proper care and
attention.


The Ideal Aquarium Conditions For A Lemon Tetra


Originating from the Tapajós River Basin in South America, Lemon Tetra are best suited to heavily planted aquariums with filtered lighting and sandy substrate to mimic their natural habitat. This little yellow fish is often found in narrow streams and so enjoys slow water flow in their tanks. Be sure to provide safe hiding places on the perimeter with twisted roots and driftwood, with open space for swimming.


Please Note: All fish shipments have a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee when shipping with FedEx Overnight (1 Day) option. All other options void our Live Arrival Guarantee for fish. 


What We Feed Our Tetras

Tetras are naturally omnivorous, feeding predominantly on small invertebrates,
crustacea, algae, and fruit. We feed a mix of live foods including live baby brine shrimp, blackworms, bloodworms, and Daphnia. We supplement their diet with Northfin Veggie, Community, and Bug Pro to ensure a balanced and nutritious diet.

 

Our Tank Parameters

  • pH: 7.2 to 7.5
  • Gh: 8 to 16
  • Kh:  4 to 7
  • TDS: 180 to 250
  • Temp: 70 to 74

products.body_html:

The Licorice Gourami is an extremely peaceful and timid fish. It has brilliant blue  and dark brown fins with a cream-colored horizontal body striping. Under the right lighting, they display some amazing colors. They prefer heavily planted tanks as they are originally found in blackwater swamps and ditches in Bangka, Indonesia.

Please Note: All fish shipments have a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee when shipping with Overnight (1 Day) option. All other options void our Live Arrival Guarantee for fish. 

Licorice Gourami Fish

The scientific name for Licorice Gouramis is Parosphromenus deissneri.

We feed our Licorice Gourami's live baby brine. They require a meaty food while prefer live foods such as black worms or baby brine. These fish can be rather picky with their diet and don't do well with dry foods.

products.title: Knight Goby | Stigmatogobius sadanundio
products.body_html:
Knight Goby | Fan Dance Goby

The Knight Goby (also called the Fan Goby) is a medium-sized fish known for its striking patterns. Native to the Indo-Pacific, it lives in estuaries and mangrove swamps, preferring fresh or lightly brackish waters over full saltwater. In home aquariums it adapts well to different conditions but thrives in moderately hard freshwater. These gobies can be somewhat territorial—dominant individuals may chase others—so keeping them in larger groups with plenty of hiding spots helps prevent bullying. Hardy and visually unique, they do best in species-only tanks or with larger fish, as they will likely eat anything small enough to fit in their mouth.

We also feed our Gobys prepared algae wafers and let them graze on natural algae and bio-film. This can be supplemented by using Bacter A.E. which can be found by clicking here.

These peaceful mollies get along well with most other tankmates and love to explore plants and decor. Their hardy fish, meaning they stay healthy in a wide range of pH levels and temperatures, making them a good choice for beginner hobbyists.

 

We give each Molly at least two weeks of expert care before shipping, then pack them in breather bags inside recycled‑foam boxes.

products.body_html:
The Gold Ring Danio

The Gold Ring Danio is a great addition to any hobbyists aquarium, planted or unplanted. The Gold Ring Danio has great color contrast displaying gold/tan coloration with black spots on its sides. They show schooling behavior in small groups yet this behavior is very evident in larger groups of 10 or more.

They are will do well in small tanks from 10 gallons and up. The reason they are a great beginner fish is do to their adaptability to a large range of water parameters and willingness to accept a wide variety of foods. We would not recommend keeping them with shrimp if trying to grow your colony as they can prey on baby shrimp.

We feed our Gold Ring Danio's a mix of frozen foods, live foods including daphnia, baby brine and cyclops and occasionally feed them Northfin Veggie, Community and Bug Pro.

products.title: Calico Bristlenose Pleco | Ancistrus sp.
products.body_html:
Calico Bristlenose Pleco

 

The Calico Bristlenose Pleco, also known as the Bushynose or Bushymouth Pleco, is an attractive and unique addition to any aquarium. This member of the Loricariidae family is a South American suckermouth catfish that grows to a size of 4 to 6 inches (10 to 16 cm), making it an ideal choice for most aquariums.

Unlike other pleco species, the Calico Bristlenose Pleco is a generally peaceful and hardy fish that requires minimal care. It is an excellent scavenger and will help to control algae in the aquarium while eating excess food. Its facial area becomes littered with bristle-like appendages beginning in sub-adulthood, which is more pronounced in males, giving it a unique appearance and receiving the names Bristlenose, Bushynose or Bushymouth Pleco. 

The Calico Pleco is primarily nocturnal but will become active during daytime hours once established in an aquarium. Providing plenty of hiding places with driftwood and rockwork is essential for this pleco to thrive. It will benefit from wood in it's home by feeding on the natural bio-film and in some cases consuming the wood as it breaks down in the water.

The Calico Bristlenose Pleco is an omnivore, but its diet should be heavy in vegetable matter, including high-quality vegetable flakes, sinking algae wafers, and pellets. It will also appreciate being fed fresh vegetables such as shelled peas, spinach, zucchini, and cucumber. Occasional meaty foods such as bloodworms are also beneficial when fed sparingly.

This fish is generally solitary but will socialize during breeding. We prefer to keep two male plecos per one female pleco for breeding. This will give males a break between litters since the males are the ones whom guard and care for the eggs. Males may be somewhat territorial with each other, but disputes can usually be diffused by providing ample aquarium space and pleco caves. All other tankmates will be generally ignored, although it is possible that small shrimp might fall prey to the Calico Bristlenose Pleco.

Overall, the Calico Bristlenose Pleco is a very practical and manageable fish that will make an excellent addition to any aquarium. Tank parameters for this fish include a pH level range of 6.5-7.5, a temperature range of 72°-86°F (22°-30°C), and a minimum tank size of 10 gallons (for breeding purposes, we prefer a 20 gallon long). So, if you're looking for a peaceful and unique fish that will help keep your aquarium clean and attractive, the Calico Bristlenose Pleco is an excellent choice.

Our Food Recommendation for Bristlenose Plecos: 

NorthFin Kelp Wafers
NorthFin Veggie
NorthFin Community

Our Tank Parameters

  • pH: 7.2 to 7.5
  • Gh: 8 to 16
  • Kh:  4 to 7
  • TDS: 180 to 250
  • Temp: 70 to 74

Tank Recommendations

  • Leaves: Indian Almond or Guava
  • Wood: Malaysian or Mopani
  • Cones: Alder Cones

products.title: Bumblebee Platy
products.body_html:
The Bumble Bee Platy

The Bumblebee Platy or Xiphophorus maculatus is a stunning variation of Platy, instantly distinguishable by its bright yellow body with black markings. Platyfish are livebearers so be mindful of mixed sexed groups as they are prolific breeders. 

For whom is the Bumblebee Platy suited?

The Bumblebee Platy is suited for both beginner and experienced aquarists thanks to their hardy nature, friendly temperament, and ease of care. Small and conveniently sized, they can thrive in both small and large tanks and are safe to pair with other peaceful fish.

What are they like?

Platyfish are highly active, friendly fish who love to investigate their surroundings and
therefore could disturb any docile tankmates. Their inquisitive nature and active lifestyle make them fun to observe and enjoyable to keep. The Bumblebee Platy is best kept with groups as a naturally schooling fish, and looks particularly impressive when swimming as a unit thanks to their vibrant colors.

The ideal conditions for the Bumblebee Platy

Native Central America and Mexico, the Bumblebee Platy prefers a heavily planted aquarium with a sandy substrate filled with lush flora and floating plants to provide comfortable hiding spots. Platyfish typically occupy all levels of the tank but enjoy swimming in the open water, so provide enough space for regular activity. Platyfish aren’t afraid to jump, so ensure a tight lid is fitted in case they decide to escape.

Please Note: All fish shipments have a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee when shipping with Overnight (1 Day) option. All other options void our Live Arrival Guarantee for fish. 

What we feed Platyfish

We feed our fish a mix of frozen, live and prepared foods including live baby brine and black worms. We also supplement their diet with a mix of Northfin Veggie, Community and Bug Pro. 

Our Tank Parameters

  • Gh: 6 to 18
  • Kh:  2 to 6
  • TDS: 100 to 200

products.title: Bleeding Blue Tetra | Hyphessobrycon Margitae
products.body_html:
Tetra - Bleeding Blue

The Bleeding Blue Tetra - a true jewel of the aquatic world!

The Bleeding Blue Tetra (scientifically known as Hyphessobrycon Margitae) boasts a vibrant and iridescent blue hue, adorned with striking red patches on its fins, truly resembling a living work of art. Its eye-catching coloration is further enhanced by the shimmering effect created by the light dancing on its scales, captivating both seasoned aquarists and newcomers alike.

This small-sized tetra is native to the pristine waters of South America, where it thrives in peaceful and well-maintained environments. Its calm and non-aggressive temperament makes it an excellent choice for community tanks, where it can coexist harmoniously with other compatible fish species.

Beyond its captivating appearance, the Bleeding Blue Tetra is cherished for its ease of care. It adapts well to a variety of water conditions, making it an ideal choice for aquarium enthusiasts of all skill levels. To ensure the best possible care for these delightful creatures, we recommend maintaining a well-balanced tank with proper filtration, regular water changes, and a suitable diet.

Feeding the Bleeding Blue Tetra is a delight in itself, as these fish are omnivorous and relish a diverse diet. Providing them with high-quality flake food, supplemented with occasional live or frozen treats such as brine shrimp and daphnia, will keep them healthy, vibrant, and active.

With their playful and inquisitive nature, the Bleeding Blue Tetras are sure to entertain you with their synchronized schooling behavior. Watching them glide gracefully through the water in unison is a sight to behold, truly mesmerizing both young and old.

At Flip Aquatics, we take great care in sourcing our Bleeding Blue Tetras from reputable breeders, ensuring that they are healthy and acclimated before reaching your aquarium. We are committed to providing you with the finest quality fish and outstanding customer service to support you on your aquatic journey.

Add a touch of elegance and serenity to your aquarium with the enchanting Bleeding Blue Tetra. Discover the joy of witnessing these beautiful creatures thrive in your underwater oasis. Order yours today and experience the captivating allure of these aquatic gems!

Please Note: All fish shipments have a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee when shipping with Overnight (1 Day) option. All other options void our Live Arrival Guarantee for fish. 

To replicate their natural habitat, the use of rock work, plants, and driftwood should be included in the aquarium. They also enjoy tannins in their water, so the use of Indian Almond or Guava leaves is highly recommended. We would not recommend keeping this fish with shrimp colonies due to the likeliness of the bleeding blue tetra eating baby shrimp and potentially picking at adult shrimp.

They are omnivores by nature. We feed our Bleeding Blue Tetras a mix of frozen foods including daphnia, baby brine, cyclops, and bloodworms with the occassional mix of Northfin foods. 

Our Tank Parameters

  • Temp: 72 to 78

Tank Recommendations

products.title: Willow Moss | Fontinalis antipyretica
products.body_html:
Willow Moss

Fontinalis antipyretica, also known as Willow Moss, is the epitome of natural elegance for your aquarium or terrarium. Its delicate, feather-like fronds and vibrant green coloration create a mesmerizing underwater scene reminiscent of a tranquil forest stream. Beyond its aesthetic appeal, Fontinalis antipyretica serves a practical role in aquarium ecosystems, providing hiding spots for small fish and invertebrates while oxygenating the water and maintaining a healthy balance by absorbing excess nutrients.

Perfect for both novice and experienced aquarists, Fontinalis antipyretica is a low-maintenance plant that thrives in freshwater and brackish environments alike. Easily anchored to driftwood, rocks, or substrate, this versatile moss spreads quickly, transforming your aquatic setup into a captivating underwater paradise.

Scientific Name: Fontinalis Antipyretica 

Sold As: Portion Cup (Please note some fronds may be black, these can still sprout new fronds of the moss so use all of it.)

Care Level: Medium

Lighting Requirement: Low

Co2: None

Growth Rate: Medium

Origin: Europe

Tank Placement: Foreground-midground

Most plants are sold as trimmings from rooted plants unless otherwise stated. All trimmings will be at least 3" in height unless otherwise noted.

products.title: Chinese Algae Eater | Gyrinocheilus aymonieri
products.body_html:
The Chinese Algae Eater Fish

Meet the Chinese Algae Eater, also known as the Gyrinocheilus aymonieri – a stunning addition to your aquarium. With its distinctive golden hue and black lateral line, this fish not only adds visual appeal but excels as a natural algae control agent. Growing up to 11 inches, these hardy and low-maintenance algae eaters are perfect for both beginners and experienced aquarists. Their peaceful temperament makes them compatible with various community fish, and their energetic swimming patterns bring life to your tank. Elevate your aquatic environment with the beauty and functionality of the Gyrinocheilus aymonieri, a standout choice for any freshwater setup.

 

When adding Chinese Algae Eaters, it's generally recommended to keep them in small groups of 3 to 5 individuals. This helps reduce stress and encourages more natural behaviors in the group. However, it's essential to consider the size of your aquarium and its compatibility with other fish species as they can get up to 11 inches in size. Ensure that the tank is spacious enough to accommodate the group comfortably and that there are suitable hiding places to create a harmonious environment. Monitoring the interactions among the Chinese Algae Eaters and other tank inhabitants is crucial to prevent territorial disputes and maintain a peaceful community.

 

The Chinese Algae Eater consumes many natural food sources but will accept prepared foods with lots of vegetable matter such as kelp wafers. It is good practice to provide fresh cut vegetables such as cucumber and zucchini.

products.title: Melanistius Cory | Black Sail Cory
products.body_html:
Melanistius Cory | Black Sail Cory

The melanistius cory (Corydoras melanistius), also known as the black sail cory or bluespotted cory, is a small, peaceful freshwater fish ideal for community aquariums. Native to parts of South America, this species thrives in well-maintained tanks with soft substrates, growing to about 2.2 inches (5.5 cm). The melanistius cory has a lifespan of 5 to 10 years and does well in groups of six or more. It is a great fit for community tanks, coexisting peacefully with other non-aggressive species.

Please Note: All fish shipments have a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee

How Big Do Melanistius Corys Get?

The melanistius cory, also known as the black sail or bluespotted cory, typically reaches a size of about 2.2 inches (5.5 cm) when fully grown. This makes it an excellent choice for medium-sized aquariums.

What's the Lifespan of the Melanistius Cory?

Under optimal conditions, the melanistius cory can live between 5 and 10 years. Proper water quality and care will help ensure the longevity of this fish in your aquarium.

Are Black Sail Corys Good for Community Aquariums?

Yes! The Black Sail Cory is peaceful and thrives in community tanks, especially with non-aggressive species like small characins and other peaceful catfish. Ideally, it should be kept in groups of six or more to encourage natural behaviors.

We feed our Corys a large variety of foods which include finely ground Northfin flake foods, small Northfin pellets (veggie, community, & bug pro), frozen foods, and occasionally blackworms.

products.title: Dojo Loach | Misgurnus Anguillicaudatus
products.body_html:
Weather Loach

The Dojo Loach, often referred to as the Weather Loach, because people say they can predict the weather. When the air pressure around them changes, like before a storm or even on a sunny day, then they start wiggling and swimming more. This happens because their bodies can sense even the tiniest shifts in the water as the air pressure rises or falls. It’s almost like they’re telling you, “Rain is coming” or “The sky will clear up soon”

These loaches can grow as long up to about ten inches, so they need a long tank with soft sand or fine gravel to dig and explore. They accept a wide range of water temps between 59-77F. Dojo Loaches are peaceful and enjoy being in small groups, where you can watch them burrow together and play in the sand.

We care for every Dojo Loach in our tanks for at least 14+ days before shipping. When it’s time to ship them to you, we bag each fish in a breather bag to prevent sloshing, tuck them into boxes lined with eco‑foam, and ship it overnight. 

Every order comes with our 100% live arrival guarantee. If your Dojo Loach doesn’t arrive healthy and active, just let us know and we’ll make it right. We’re here to help you before, during, and after your purchase so both you and your new loaches can be happy.

Please Note: Dojo loaches are illegal to own and purchase in Illinois, Michigan, Minnesota, and New York. We urge you to be responsible and refrain from releasing any aquatic life into local waterways.

products.title: Blue Velvet Shrimp
products.body_html:
Blue Velvet shrimp

Blue Velvet shrimp are light blue neocaridina shrimp that were actually created from Red Rili Neocaridina. These shrimp offer another beautiful blue variation to any Neocaridina tank. One of the coolest things about them is that when they’re berried, they carry bright green eggs, which really stand out against their blue color! Blue Velvet Shrimp thrive in general Neocaridina parameters with a pH above 7. Any inert substrate will work in their tank. Providing natural cover and surface area for biofilm to grow with wood, leaves, and cones will benefit these shrimp and their offspring. Mosses and other live plants should be included in their tank setup. They will eat any shrimp-specific manufactured foods, biofilm, and algae.

products.title: Amazon Frogbit
products.body_html:
Amazon Frogbit - Floating Aquarium Plant

Amazon Frogbit (Limnobium laevigatum) is a floating plant that is equally suitable for both open aquariums and aquariums with a cover. Amazon Frogbit has large bright green leaves and long finely branched roots, the Frogbit not only provides excellent protection for young fish but is also an eye-catcher in any aquarium. Many well-known ornamental fish such as barbs, tetras, and egg-laying toothcarps love Frogbit as an ideal spawning substrate. Here the eggs can develop in peace, and the young fry are protected from predators.

Is Amazon Frogbit Beginner Friendly?

As far as floating plants go, Amazon Frogbit is an excellent plant for both beginner and more experienced aquarists. Frogbit's main requirement is sufficient light to grow optimally, which generally isn't difficult to achieve with floating plants.

What is Frogbit and How Fast does Frogbit Grow?

Amazon Frogbit is quite fast-growing. As a result, Frogbit consumes excess nutrients and prevents excessive algae development. Furthermore, the shade provided by the large leaves of the Amazon Frogbit contributes considerably to keeping algae in the aquarium in check. Although Frogbit reproduces quite rapidly through the numerous offshoots that appear, excess specimens of Frogbit can be easily removed.

Amazon Frogbit Water Parameters

Frogbit is very tolerant of water temperature and grows well in the range of 62 to even 95°F. Soft to medium-hard water is favorable. Good growth is supported by intensive lighting and high humidity. When the water level is low, Amazon Frogbit develops particularly well, because the roots are able to reach the substrate. Under favorable conditions, Frogbit multiplies quite quickly and form a dense floating plant cover, the extent of which can be regulated very easily by collecting excess plants. 

*All Plants may contain snails or snail eggs. We keep them in all of our tanks for the benefits they provide.*

products.title: Mix + Match Anubias Bundle
products.body_html:
Anubias Bundle

products.title: Dwarf Red Flamingo Guppy
products.body_html:
Red Flamingo Guppy

The Red Flamingo Guppy is a beautiful freshwater fish with vibrant red and orange tones that brighten any aquarium. Known for their easy care and peaceful nature, flamingo guppies are perfect for beginners and community tanks. The Flamingo Guppy thrives in a stable, well-maintained tank with slightly warm water and enjoys swimming in groups.

These active, eye-catching fish add color and movement to your setup. The flamingo red guppy pairs well with other peaceful fish, making them a popular choice for aquarists of all levels.

Our Tank Parameters:

  • Ph: 7.2-8.2
  • Gh: 8-12
  • Kh: 4-6
  • TDS: 150-200
  • Temp: 72-80
Please note that when shipping with the Overnight (1 Day) option, we provide a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee for fish shipments. All other shipping options void our Live Arrival Guarantee.

Frequently Asked Questions:

1. What size tank do Red Flamingo Guppies need?
A 10-gallon tank or larger is ideal for these active swimmers, especially when kept in groups.

2. Is the Flamingo Guppy easy to care for?
Yes, flamingo guppies are hardy and beginner-friendly, requiring clean water, stable temperatures, and a balanced diet.

3. Can I keep a Red Flamingo Guppy with other fish?
Absolutely! They are peaceful and do well with other small, non-aggressive species like tetras or corydoras.

4. What does a Flamingo Red Guppy eat?
They enjoy a varied diet, including high-quality flakes, micro-pellets, and occasional live or frozen foods like brine shrimp.

5. How big do Flamingo Guppies grow?
Males typically reach about 1.5 inches, while females grow slightly larger, up to 2 inches.

products.title: Bleeding Heart Tetra | Hyphessobrycon Socolofi
products.body_html:
Bleeding Heart Tetra

Are you searching for an exquisite and captivating addition to your freshwater aquarium? Look no further than the Bleeding Heart Tetra (Hyphessobrycon Socolofi). This remarkable fish species is renowned for its striking appearance and peaceful demeanor, making it an ideal choice for both novice and experienced aquarists. Let's explore what makes the Bleeding Heart Tetra a must-have for your aquatic collection.

Appearance: The Bleeding Heart Tetra gets its name from the distinctive heart-shaped red mark that adorns its silvery body. This unique and eye-catching feature makes it stand out among other aquarium fish. With translucent fins and a delicate body, these tetras are a true marvel to behold. Under the right conditions, their colors can intensify, creating a visual spectacle in your aquarium.

Size and Compatibility: These tetras are relatively small, typically reaching an adult size of 1.5 to 2 inches (3.8 to 5 cm). Their diminutive size makes them an excellent choice for smaller aquariums, but they also thrive in larger setups. Due to their peaceful nature, Bleeding Heart Tetras are compatible with a wide range of tankmates, including other community fish like danios, rasboras, and gouramis. Their social behavior makes them a great addition to a community aquarium.

Tank Requirements: To keep your Bleeding Heart Tetras happy and healthy, provide them with a well-maintained aquarium. A tank of at least 20 gallons is recommended, with ample hiding places created by plants and driftwood. These tetras appreciate a densely planted tank with soft, slightly acidic water conditions, mimicking their natural habitat in South America.

Feeding Habits: Bleeding Heart Tetras are omnivorous and enjoy a varied diet. Offer them a mix of high-quality flakes, pellets, and live or frozen foods like brine shrimp, daphnia, and bloodworms. Providing a balanced diet will help maintain their vibrant colors and overall well-being.

Behavior: One of the most endearing qualities of Bleeding Heart Tetras is their peaceful temperament. They thrive in a community setting and are known for their playful and active behavior. These social fish often school together, creating a visually stunning spectacle in your aquarium.

Breeding: Breeding Bleeding Heart Tetras can be a rewarding experience for hobbyists. To encourage breeding, provide them with soft, slightly acidic water, and plenty of plants for egg-laying. A separate breeding tank may be necessary to protect the eggs and fry from potential predators. With the right conditions, you may soon witness the hatching of tiny fry, adding a new generation of these stunning fish to your collection.

In conclusion, the Bleeding Heart Tetra is a captivating and peaceful addition to any freshwater aquarium. With its striking appearance, ease of care, and social behavior, it's an excellent choice for both beginners and experienced aquarists. Bring the beauty and charm of the Bleeding Heart Tetra into your aquatic world today and enjoy the splendor it brings to your underwater haven.

Note: All fish shipments have a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee when shipping with Overnight (1 Day) option. All other options void our Live Arrival Guarantee for fish.   

Our Tank Parameters

  • Ph: 6.0 to 8.0
  • Gh:8 to 16
  • Kh: 4 to 7
  • TDS:180 to 250
  • Temp:70 to 80

Tank Recommendations

  • Plants: Middle to top tank plant species
  • Leaves: Indian Almond or Guava
  • Wood: Mopani or Malaysian
  • Cones: Alder Cones
  • Other: Active substrate to lower pH

products.title: Panda Endler
products.body_html:
Panda Endlers

Panda Endlers are colorful, freshwater fish, with black and white markings and hints of iridescent color. These small, lively fish are closely related to guppies and share their active nature and hardiness, making them great for both beginner and experienced hobbyists.

Panda Endlers thrive in groups and enjoy swimming in the middle and upper parts of the tank. They are peaceful fish, ideal for community tanks with other non-aggressive species. Easy to care for, they prefer a well-maintained tank with stable water conditions, live plants, and a diet of quality flakes, small pellets, and the occasional treat like brine shrimp.

Our Tank Parameters

  • pH: 7.2-8.2
  • Gh: 8-12
  • Kh: 4-6
  • TDS: 150-200
  • Temp: 72-80
Please Note: All fish shipments have a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee when shipping with
Overnight (1 Day) option. All other options void our Live Arrival Guarantee for fish. 

Frequently Asked Questions:

1. How big do Panda Endlers get?

Panda Endlers are small fish, typically growing to about 1 to 1.5 inches in length, making them perfect for nano or community tanks.

2. What tank size is best for Panda Endlers?

A tank of at least 5 gallons is suitable for a small group of Panda Endlers, but a 10-gallon tank or larger is ideal for providing more space and encouraging active swimming.

3. Are Panda Endlers good for beginners?

Yes! Panda Endlers are hardy, adaptable, and easy to care for, making them a great choice for beginners.

4. Can Panda Endlers live with other fish?

Yes, they are peaceful and get along well with other small, non-aggressive species like tetras, guppies, or Corydoras. Avoid housing them with larger or aggressive fish.

5. What do Panda Endlers eat?

Panda Endlers are omnivorous and enjoy a varied diet of high-quality flakes, micro pellets, and occasional treats like brine shrimp or daphnia to enhance their colors. We typically recommend Northfin food

6. Do Panda Endlers breed easily?

Yes, Panda Endlers are livebearers and reproduce readily in aquariums. If males and females are housed together, you’ll likely see fry (baby fish) appear in no time!

7. What makes Panda Endlers unique?

Their striking black-and-white patterns, energetic behavior, and small size make them a standout choice for nano tanks or as an eye-catching addition to a larger community aquarium.

8. How many Panda Endlers should I keep together?

It’s best to keep them in groups of at least six, as they are social fish and feel more comfortable in schools.

products.title: Neon Kuhli Loach | Pangio kuhlii
products.body_html:
Neon Kuhli Loach

The Neon Kuhli Loach has a slender, eel-like body with a mix of iridescent blues and oranges, creating a stunning contrast against a dark substrate or lush plant life. The Neon Kuhli Loach is a peaceful and social bottom-dweller, making it a great companion for other non-aggressive species in a community tank.

In addition to its striking appearance, the Neon Kuhli Loach is known for its active and inquisitive behavior. Often seen darting in and out of crevices or burrowing into the substrate, it adds a lively and playful energy to the aquarium. This loach thrives in well-maintained tanks with soft, sandy substrates and plenty of hiding spots, such as driftwood or densely planted areas. 

What are they like?

The Kuhli Loach is a real night owl. They are active mainly at night and can peacefully co-exist with most other fish. During the night, they will look for food that sunk to the bottom of the aquarium. On top of that, their natural curiosity will drive them to explore any crevices and caves that might be around in their habitat.

The ideal conditions for the Kuhli Loach

Despite the fact that these fish are scavengers, they still require a relatively clean, well-oxygenated tank. They highly prefer a soft substrate, as it makes searching for food more manageable, so sand or fine gravel would be best. The water should be soft, slightly acidic and at a temperature of 73 – 86 °F, as these are tropical fish. Further, you should make sure that there is moderate lighting in their habitat.

Please Note: All fish shipments have a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee when shipping with Overnight (1 Day) option. All other options void our Live Arrival Guarantee for fish. 

Our Tank Parameters:

  • Ph: 6.5 to 7.1
  • Gh: 8 to 12
  • Kh:  2 to 4
  • Temp: 72 to 80

    Tank Recommendations:

    • Rock: Smooth rock (round in shape)
    • Leaves: Indian Almond or Guava
    • Cones: Alder, Giant Alder, Birch or Casuarina 
    • Plants: Java Ferns, Anubias, Sagittaria, Cryptocorene

products.title: Full Red Guppy | Poecilia Reticulatus
products.body_html:
Red Guppy

The Red Guppy, also referred to as the full red guppy, is a striking freshwater fish known for its uniform red coloring across its body and fins. Its albino trait, identifiable by its pale body and red eyes, makes this guppy unique. The red guppy is small, with males typically showing more intense colors and larger, flowing fins compared to females.

Red guppies are hardy and easy to care for, making them a good choice for beginners and seasoned aquarists. They do well in peaceful community tanks with stable water conditions. A varied diet of flakes, pellets, and live or frozen foods helps maintain their health and color. These guppies are active swimmers and breed readily, making them a lively addition to aquariums.

Our Tank Parameters:

  • Ph: 7.2-8.2
  • Gh: 8-12
  • Kh: 4-6
  • TDS: 150-200
  • Temp: 72-80
Please Note: All fish shipments have a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee when shipping with
Overnight (1 Day) option. All other options void our Live Arrival Guarantee for fish. 

1. What do Red Guppies eat?

These guppies are omnivores and benefit from a diet of high-quality flakes or pellets, supplemented with live or frozen foods like brine shrimp, daphnia, and bloodworms. This varied diet helps maintain their health and vibrant coloration.

2. Are Full Red Guppies good for community tanks?

Yes, they are peaceful and do well with other small, non-aggressive fish. Compatible tank mates include tetras, rasboras, and Corydoras catfish.

3. How can I encourage breeding in Red Guppies?

To encourage breeding, maintain optimal water conditions, provide plenty of hiding spots for fry (such as plants or breeding mops), and feed high-protein foods. Females typically give birth to live fry every 4-6 weeks.

4. What is the average lifespan of Red Guppies?

With proper care, these guppies can live 2-3 years. Regular water changes, a balanced diet, and stress-free conditions help maximize their lifespan.

5. How can I distinguish males from females?

Males are smaller and display brighter, more vivid red coloration, along with longer and more elaborate fins. Females are larger, have a more rounded body, and less intense coloration.

products.title: Diamond Head Neon Tetra (Blue Diamond)
products.body_html:
Live Blue Diamond Neon Tetra

The Diamond Head Neon Tetra (also known as Blue Diamond Neon Tetra) is a variation of one of the aquarium hobbies most popular schooling fish, the neon tetra. The biggest difference between the two fish is that the Diamond Head does not have a blue strip down it's side. Instead, the Blue Diamond Neon Tetra has a bright blue diamond on it's forehead (between their eyes). Like most tetras, these fish prefer to be kept in schools of 5 or more in order to make them feel the most comfortable.

Please Note: All fish shipments have a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee when shipping with Overnight (1 Day) option. All other options void our Live Arrival Guarantee for fish. 

The Neon Tetra originally came from the streams of South America. By selective breeding, the Blue Diamond Neon Tetra was developed. To replicate their natural habitat, the use of rock work, plants, and driftwood should be included in the aquarium. We would not recommend keeping this fish with shrimp colonies due to the likeliness of the Blue Diamond Neon Tetra eating baby shrimp and potentially picking at adult shrimp.

The scientific name for the  Diamond Head Neon Tetra is Paracheirodon innesi var. "Blue Diamond"

We feed our Diamond Head Neon Tetras a mix of frozen foods including daphnia, baby brine, cyclops, and Bloodworms. We also feed a mix of Northfin flake foods, Northfin Community / Veggie pellets and live black worms. 

Our Tank Parameters

  • Ph: 7.2 to 7.8
  • Gh: 6 to 12
  • Kh:  2 to 6
  • TDS: 120 to 200
  • Temp: 72 to 86

Tank Recommendations

  • Leaves: Indian Almond or Guava
  • Wood: Mopani or Malaysian
  • Cones: Alder Cones
  • Other: Buffered substrate to lower pH, not required
  • Live Plants: Any tall plants would work to make them feel safe

products.title: Bloodfin Tetra | Aphyocharax anisitsi
products.body_html:
Tetra Bloodfin Fish

Bloodfin Tetras are small, peaceful fish that do best in groups of six or more. They have clear to silvery bodies and bright, blood‑red fins—that’s how they got their name. These tetras can be a bit shy at first, so you’ll often see them darting together near plants or decorations for safety.

Like most tetras, Bloodfin Tetras are easy to care for. Keep your tank temperature between 72–79°F and the pH around 6.5–7.5. They’ll eat high‑quality flakes, pellets, and small live or frozen foods like daphnia or brine shrimp.

products.title: Tiger Teddy | Neoheterandria elegans
products.body_html:
Tiger Teddy

The Tiger Teddy (Neoheterandria elegans) is one of the smallest livebearers available in the aquarium hobby! It has tiger-like stripes and a very compact size with males reaching only 0.7 inches and females 0.98 inches. Fry from these fish are incredibly small making these a fun addition to any hobbyist's fish collection. This species captivates with its unique appearance and lively demeanor. Native to the rivers of Central America, the Tiger Teddy is well-suited for nano aquariums and community tanks, where it thrives in groups and interacts peacefully with other small, gentle species. Its small size and vibrant patterns make it an ideal choice for aquarists looking to add a touch of exotic flair to their setup.

Beyond its visual appeal, the Tiger Teddy is a livebearer, dropping a few fry everyday over several days. Despite its delicate appearance, this fish is surprisingly hardy, adapting well to a variety of water conditions. It also contributes to the overall balance of your aquarium by grazing on small algae and detritus. 

What We Feed Them-

We feed our fish a mix of frozen, live, and prepared foods including live baby brine and black worms. We also supplement their diet with a mix of Northfin Veggie, Community, and Bug Pro.

The Banded Limia thrives in a community tank setting, displaying a peaceful temperament that makes it compatible with a variety of tankmates. Its hardy nature and adaptability to a range of water conditions make it an ideal choice for both beginner and experienced aquarists.

In addition to its visual appeal, the Banded Limia offers several benefits to your aquarium. As livebearers, they give birth to free-swimming fry, adding to the community of a tank. They also actively engage in natural algae control, grazing on various types of algae that may develop in the tank. This species is known for its social behavior, often forming small schools that create an engaging display as they interact with one another. 

What We Feed Them-

products.title: Humpback Limia | Limia nigrofasciata
products.body_html:
Humpback Limia | Rare Livebearer

The Humpback Limia (Limia nigrofasciata) has a distinctive arched back and striking vertical black bars, this species stands out as a true conversation piece in any tank. Native to the waters of Haiti and the Dominican Republic, the Humpback Limia is known for its hardy nature and adaptability, making it an excellent choice for aquarists of all experience levels. This peaceful fish thrives in community tanks, where it can interact harmoniously with other species.

In addition to its visual appeal, the Humpback Limia is a livebearer, providing the exciting opportunity to observe the natural process of giving birth to free-swimming fry. This species is also an active grazer, helping to control algae growth and maintain a clean, balanced aquarium environment. Its social nature often leads to the formation of small schools, creating a dynamic and lively display in your tank.

What We Feed Them-

We feed our fish a mix of frozen, live, and prepared foods including live baby brine and black worms. We also supplement their diet with a mix of Northfin Veggie, Community, and Bug Pro.

  • Gh: 6 to 18
  • Kh:  2 to 6
  • TDS: 100 to 200
  • Temp: 72 to 80

products.title: Golden Wonder Killifish | Aplocheilus lineatus
products.body_html:
Golden Wonder Killifish

The Golden Wonder Killifish, Aplocheilus lineatus, is a striking freshwater fish known for its vibrant golden-yellow coloration and elongated, torpedo-shaped body. Growing up to 4 inches in length, this surface-dwelling species is an eye-catching addition to any aquarium. Its shimmering scales reflect light beautifully, creating a captivating display that draws attention from every angle.

The Golden Wonder Killifish is relatively easy to care for and can thrive in a well-maintained community tank, provided its tank mates are of similar size and temperament. It prefers a planted aquarium with plenty of floating vegetation to mimic its natural habitat. 

Please Note: All fish shipments have a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee when shipping with FedEx Overnight (1 Day) option. All other options void our Live Arrival Guarantee for fish. 

We feed these killifish an exclusive diet of live brine shrimp, frozen daphnia and cyclops.

Our Tank Parameters

  • Gh: 8 to 16
  • Kh:  4 to 7
  • TDS: 180 to 250
  • Temp: 68 to 79
