products.title: Fritz - KH Liquid Test Kit
products.body_html:
Alkalinity Test Kit | Fritz

Fritz Liquid Test Kits

  • Easy to Read Instructions

  • Essential for a Healthy Tank

  • Accurate in both Fresh and Saltwater

Carbonate hardness, or alkalinity (KH), is the measure of both carbonates and bicarbonates present in the water. The KH value of your water is important to know, as the carbonate hardness plays a significant role in the water’s pH and the water’s buffering capacity to keep that pH stable. KH naturally falls over time with the introduction of CO2 and other acids, and nitrification. Reverse osmosis water should be buffered with carbonates before use to raise and maintain KH. Some municipal tap water can have variable KH levels throughout the year and should be checked regularly. In saltwater tanks, KH can be tested to monitor the proper ionic balance when dosing.

Dosage / Instructions

To remove childproof safety cap, push down while turning.

  1. Fill a clean test tube with 5 ml of water to be tested (to the line on the tube).
  2. Holding KH Reagent Solution dropper bottle upside down in a completely vertical position add one drop at a time. Cap the test tube and gently shake between drops. Ensure you count the drops as they are added.
  3. The test is complete when the water in the test tube, after having been gently shaken, turns from blue to yellow. If you have difficulty discerning the color after the first drop of test solution is added, remove the cap from the test tube and while holding it over a white background, look down through the tube.

The KH value is determined by the number of drops of reagent that must be added to turn the water in the test tube yellow. See conversion chart to determine KH.

Corrective actions:

To increase the KH of your water, add Fritz pH Higher. For a more significant KH increase for African cichlid systems, add Fritz Rift Lake Cichlid Buffer as needed. Fritz recommends a minimum of 4 dKH (80 ppm) for nitrification when using FritzZyme 7 or FritzZyme Turbo Start 700 bacteria. 

To decrease the KH of your water, add Fritz pH Lower, or perform partial water changes.

products.title: Fritz - Fishless Fuel Ammonia
products.body_html:
Fishless Fuel | Fritz

Establishing a healthy biological filter is essential to every aquatic system. Fritz Fishless Fuel eliminates the guesswork of dry ammonium chloride powders and other ammonia sources such as fish food or other organic materials. When using Fritz Fishless Fuel, there is also no need to put any livestock in potential danger to start a system. As a pure liquid ammonia source, easily feed your nitrifying bacteria to establish a robust, healthy biological filter. The convenient dropper bottle makes dosing simple.

The Fritz Fishless Fuel formula contains 40 mg/mL TAN (total ammonia nitrogen.)

DO NOT USE FISHLESS FUEL IN ANY SYSTEM CONTAINING LIVESTOCK. DO NOT ADD ANY ANIMALS TO A SYSTEM THAT HAS MEASURABLE AMMONIA OR NITRITE.

Directions & Dosage

To bring aquarium to 2 ppm ammonia: Add 4 drops per One U.S. Gallon (3.78 L) or 1 tsp (5 ml) per 25 U.S. Gallons (94.6 L) DO NOT USE WHEN LIVESTOCK IS PRESENT.

Fritz Fishless Fuel Ammonia Solution is to be used when performing a fishless cycle. Add required amount of Fritz Fishless Fuel liquid ammonia solution to reach desired ammonia level (Fritz recommends 2-4 ppm). After adding ammonia add FritzZyme Nitrifying Bacteria and test daily. Once ammonia and nitrite levels are reading zero, livestock can be safely added. Continue to monitor ammonia and nitrite on a regular basis.

  • Use along with nitrifying bacteria (FritzZyme or TurboStart) as a food source
  • No equipment restrictions necessary
  • Odor variations are normal; some lots may not have a discernable ammonia odor
  • Contraindications: DO NOT add Ammonium Chloride to an aquarium with livestock present
  • Overdosed? Perform small water changes if needed to dilute
  • Test aquarium water with an ammonia test kit with a range up to at least 4 ppm
  • NOTE: Be careful not to use too much ammonium chloride. Raising ammonia levels above 5.0ppm may actually slow the cycle time.

products.title: Daisy's Blue Rice Killifish | Oryzias Woworae
products.body_html:
Live Blue Rice Killifish for Aquariums

The Daisy's Blue Rice Killifish is a beautiful small blue and orange fish. They only grow to 1.2”. They inhabit shallow heavily vegetated slow-moving streams with submerged branches, roots, or leaf litter.  They are active fish that look great in a small group.  They prefer warm temperatures 74-81o F. They come from one stream on the island of Muna, Indonesia.

Please Note: All fish shipments have a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee when shipping with the Overnight (1 Day) option. All other options void our Live Arrival Guarantee for fish. 

The Daisy's Blue Rice Killifish enjoys water that has been stained with tannins (Almond leaves would be perfect as cover, as well). They enjoy being housed with other small fish such as Sundadanio axelrodi, Pygmy Cory, Otocinclus, as well as Neocaridina shrimp (they will likely eat baby shrimp). Java Moss is great to give the eggs and fry cover since the adults will try to eat both eggs and fry.

Daisy's Blue Rice Killifish prefer to be kept in small groups of 8-10 individuals.  We feed them a large variety of foods which include finely ground Northfin flake foods, small Northfin pellets (veggie, community, & bug pro), frozen foods.  They also like microworms and baby brine shrimp.

Their scientific name is Oryzias woworae. 

Our Tank Parameters

  • Ph: 6.5 to 7.5
  • Gh: 6 to 15
  • Kh:  2 to 5
  • TDS: 100 to 200
  • Temp: 70 to 78

Tank Recommendations

  • Leaves: Indian Almond or Guava
  • Wood: Malaysian or Mopani
  • Cones: Alder Cones
  • Substrate: Pool Filter Sand

products.title: Green Tiger Barb
products.body_html:
Green Tiger Barb Fish

The Green Tiger Barb is a small fish with shiny green bodies marked by dark stripes like a tiger’s, with bright orange fins. They grow up to about 3 inches long and love to dart around in schools, which makes them fun to watch. Because they can be a bit nippy with fish that have long fins, it’s best to keep them with other active, sturdy tank mates or in a species only group so everyone stays happy.

These barbs do best in a medium to large tank filled with live plants and open spaces for swimming. They prefer soft, slightly acidic water that moves gently, which you can create with a low-flow filter. A group of at least 6 helps them feel secure and lets you see their natural schooling behavior. Add some smooth rocks or driftwood for hiding spots, and keep the tank brightly lit to bring out their colors. With regular water changes and a stable temperature around 75–80°F, your Green Tiger Barbs will thrive and show off their best colors.

Our Tank Parameters

  • pH: 6.0 to 7.5
  • Gh: 8 to 16
  • Kh: 4 to 8
  • TDS: 180 to 250
  • Temp: 70 to 74

products.title: Blueberry Snail | Viviparus sp.
products.body_html:
Blueberry Snails | Freshwater Snail

Blueberry Snails are stunning snails that are renowned for their blue coloration and intricate patterns, making them a striking addition to any freshwater tank or pond. They grow to a manageable size of 1-2 inches in diameter. Besides their visual appeal, Blueberry Snails are excellent algae eaters, helping to keep your tank clean and balanced by consuming algae and organic waste. As livebearers, they reproduce regularly, giving birth to small, fully-formed young every 1-3 weeks, ensuring a continuous and fascinating display of snail life.

Easy to care for, Blueberry Snails are peaceful and non-aggressive, making them a perfect fit for community tanks. They require minimal maintenance, but regular monitoring of water quality and occasional feeding with algae wafers or blanched vegetables will keep them healthy. These snails are shipped securely to ensure they arrive in great condition.

We keep these snails on pool filtered sand and in bare bottom tanks with a Matten filters. We feed NorthFin Foods along with Indian Almond Leaves for natural biofilm. We also use LED lighting to provide enough natural algae growth  for them to feed. 


Please note: During shipping, snails sometimes go into a hibernation-like state and may not become fully active for up to a week once acclimated to your aquarium. 

 

Tank Parameters

  • pH: 7.2 to 8.5
  • Gh: 7 to 15
  • Kh:  2 to 8
  • TDS: 120 to 400
  • Temp: 68 to 78

Tank Equipment

  • Water: RO/DI Water remineralized with Salty Shrimp Gh/Kh+

products.title: Pristella Tetra (X-Ray) | Pristella maxillaris
products.body_html:
X-Ray Tetra

If you’re looking for something a little different, choose a Pristella Tetra or Pristella maxillaris. These striking fish are hard to miss and are guaranteed to make a splash in any community tank. Their remarkable translucent body has coined them the alternative nickname of “X-Ray Tetra," and with black, white and neon yellow striped fins they are a truly memorable fish.


What Are Pristella Tetra Like?

Pristella Tetras are known for their beautiful appearance and low-maintenance care
requirements. These little fish have vibrant personalities and a peaceful temperament, great for pairing with similar-sized tankmates and other peaceful Tetras. Naturally schooling, Tetras prefer to live in groups of six or more and can be found exploring their environment sticking close by to their fellow Pristella Tetras.

Who Is Suited To Pristella Tetra?

A fun and hardy fish, Pristella Tetras live up to five years with proper care, and are an
excellent option for beginners and intermediate owners. Due to their migration habits,
Pristella Tetras are highly adaptable but can scare easily so ensure your tank is set up to
help reduce stress.

The Ideal Aquarium Conditions For A Pristella Tetra

Native to the coastal rivers of South America, Pristella Tetras are used to living in more
brackish waters but are highly adaptable to various water conditions. The standard tropical biotope is preferred, featuring ample rocks, driftwood, caves and plants to provide safe hiding spaces around the perimeter, with enough space in the middle for free swimming.


Please Note: All fish shipments have a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee when shipping with FedEx Overnight (1 Day) option. All other options void our Live Arrival Guarantee for fish. 


What We Feed Our Tetras

Tetras are naturally omnivorous, feeding predominantly on small invertebrates,
crustacea, algae, and fruit. We feed a mix of live foods including live baby brine shrimp, blackworms, bloodworms, and Daphnia. We supplement their diet with Northfin Veggie, Community, and Bug Pro to ensure a balanced and nutritious diet.

 

Our Tank Parameters for Tetras

  • pH: 7.2 to 7.5
  • Gh: 8 to 16
  • Kh:  4 to 7
  • TDS: 180 to 250
  • Temp: 70 to 74

products.title: Emperor Tetra | Nematobrycon Palmeri
products.body_html:
Emperor Tetra

Also known as Rainbow Tetra, the Emperor Tetra or Nematobrycon Palmeri is a beautiful multi-colored freshwater fish popularized by its shimmery body and regal appearance. Emperor Tetras grow to around 1.5 inches in size making them ideal for smaller aquariums and community tanks with smaller-sized species. Their unique coloration and prominent black stipe make a nice contrast to other fish, and you’ll be able to enjoy watching their scales shine under aquarium lighting.

What Are Emperor Like?

Emperor Tetras are a smaller-sized tropical fish characterized by their peaceful nature. Their laidback personality is incompatible with boisterous tankmates, overly active swimmers or larger species who may view them as prey. Emperor Tetras are a naturally schooling fish but are more than happy to live their days in pairs if desired. Pair with other non-violent Tetras for a peaceful tank environment.

Who Is Suited To Emperor Tetra?

Suitable for beginners and experienced aquarists, Emperor Tetras are small but mighty.
They look wonderful in community tanks but must be kept away from aggressive fish and larger companions.


The Ideal Aquarium Conditions For An Emperor Tetra

Emperor Tetras are found in quiet, slow-moving Western Colombian rivers with dense
vegetation, subdued lighting, and dark substrate. Recreate this environment using sand, gravel, and ample small-leaf vegetation for a happy and thriving Emperor Tetra. They prefer soft to moderately hard tank water and require ample room to swim without getting stuck in vegetation.


Please Note: All fish shipments have a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee when shipping with
Overnight (1 Day) option. All other options void our Live Arrival Guarantee for fish. 

What We Feed Our Tetras

Tetras are naturally omnivorous, feeding predominantly on small invertebrates,
crustacea, algae, and fruit. We feed a mix of live foods including live baby brine shrimp, blackworms, bloodworms, and Daphnia. We supplement their diet with Northfin Veggie, Community, and Bug Pro to ensure a balanced and nutritious diet.

Our Tank Parameters for Tetra

  • Ph: 6.8 to 7.2
  • Gh: 4 to 10
  • Kh:  2 to 6
  • TDS: 140 to 200
  • Temp: 72 to 78

products.title: Shell Envy Mineral Food by Shrimp Envy
products.body_html:
Shell Envy Mineral Food

Shell Envy is a specially formulated mineral-based supplement designed to promote a robust and healthy carapace in shrimp. The carapace plays a crucial role in protecting their vital organs and maintaining their appearance. As shrimp grow, they molt, shedding their old carapace to reveal a softer, new layer that needs time to harden. Providing a mineral-based supplement like Shell Envy ensures that shrimp receive the necessary nutrients to harden their new shell effectively and beautifully. It's important to avoid overfeeding, as excessive mineral intake can lead to molting problems.

Made from 100% natural and organic ingredients, Shell Envy features a high mineral content blend of kale, mulberry, nettle, dandelion, moringa, spinach, spirulina, chlorella, bee pollen, kelp, alfalfa, wheatgrass, black walnut, and other beneficial components that shrimp love. It also includes essential nutrients and minerals such as beta-glucan, calcium carbonate, montmorillonite, amino acids, trace minerals, chitin, yeast, and probiotics.

This pellet food is designed to be easy to break apart, allowing precise control over feeding amounts. It sinks quickly, and shrimp will eagerly break it apart and distribute it throughout the tank. If leftover food is found after 3-4 hours, it indicates overfeeding, so reduce the pellet size or feeding frequency.

Shell Envy is also enjoyed by snails, crayfish, and crabs, making it a versatile choice for your freshwater aquarium. For best results, we recommend feeding Shell Envy based on the guide below.

Feeding Instructions: Feed 1-2 small pellets once a week, depending on the size of your shrimp colony, as a substitute for other foods. Remove any uneaten food after 2-3 hours.

About Shrimp Envy: Shrimp Envy offers premium, all-natural plant and mineral-based shrimp foods, meticulously handmade in the United States by fellow shrimp enthusiasts. Shrimp Envy's products contain no fillers, fishmeal, or junk—just pure, wholesome ingredients. Utilizing low heat methods, Shrimp Envy preserves the nutritional integrity of their foods, ensuring each pellet retains its valuable nutrients. This also makes the pellets easy to break apart, allowing shrimp of all sizes to graze without the risk of aggression.

Size: 35g or 90g

Ingredients: mulberry, nettle, montmorillonite, kale, dandelion, moringa, spinach, spirulina, chlorella, bee pollen, kelp, alfalfa, wheatgrass, pea protein, hijiki, calcium, amino acids, beta-glucan, trace minerals, micronekton, chitin, yeast and probiotics.

products.body_html:
Growth Envy Protein Food

Growth Envy is a powerful blend of protein-rich, plant-based foods designed to support the growth and development of your shrimp. Unlike many other products that rely on meat-based proteins, Growth Envy uses plant-based proteins, offering a healthier option that is closer to their natural diet. While providing a protein-rich diet can be beneficial, it's important to feed with caution to avoid potential molting issues that could harm your shrimp's overall health. Growth Envy is especially beneficial during the breeding season, as pregnant shrimp require additional protein to support their increased nutritional needs.

This supplementary shrimp food comes in pellet form, making it easy to break apart for controlled feeding. The pellets sink gradually, allowing shrimp to disperse them around the tank, ensuring that all shrimp get a share. If leftover pieces remain after 3-4 hours, it indicates overfeeding; reduce the pellet size or decrease the feeding frequency.

products.body_html:

Shrimp Envy Food Fight is a comprehensive grazing food designed to keep your shrimp happy and healthy. Using the renowned Complete Envy recipe with added soy hull and barley, this food attracts shrimp naturally. Made with 100% natural and organic ingredients, Food Fight offers a wholesome blend including soy hull, barley, mulberry, nettle, dandelion, moringa, kale, spinach, spirulina, chlorella, bee pollen, kelp, alfalfa, wheatgrass, black walnut, and other beneficial components. Food Fight can also serve as a convenient vacation food, ensuring your shrimp are well-fed while you're away, or as a complete daily diet for your shrimp.

products.title: Leopard Endlers
products.body_html:
Leopard Endler Fish

Leopard Endlers, scientifically known as Poecilia wingei, are a vibrant and eye-catching addition to any freshwater aquarium. These small, dynamic fish are renowned for their striking leopard-like spots and vivid coloration, which can range from bright oranges and reds to electric blues and greens. Their intricate patterns and iridescent scales create a dazzling display as they dart around the tank, making them a captivating focal point in any aquatic setup. 

In addition to their stunning appearance, Leopard Endlers are known for their peaceful nature and compatibility with a wide range of tank mates, including other small fish and invertebrates. These active swimmers bring life and movement to the aquarium, while their smaller size means they do not require extensive space, making them ideal for both nano and larger community tanks.

Breeding Leopard Endlers is also a straightforward and rewarding process, as they are livebearers and can produce frequent, colorful offspring, adding to the dynamic ecosystem of your aquarium. With their combination of beauty, ease of care, and lively personality, Leopard Endlers are a delightful addition to any aquarist’s collection.

Males have longer fins and often have more vivid colors, especially around breeding. These peaceful gouramis enjoy warm water 72-80 °F and tanks with lots of plants for hiding and exploring.

We care for each Blue Paradise Gourami for 14+ days before shipping. They go into breather bags and are shipped in insulated boxes lined with recycled foam insulation, then shipped overnight so they arrive healthy and stress‑free. Every order includes a 100% live arrival guarantee.

products.title: Cobalt Blue Dwarf Goby | Stiphodon semoni
products.body_html:
Cobalt Blue Dwarf Goby

Ready to add some vibrant color to your tank? Introducing the Cobalt Blue Dwarf Goby, a stunning fish that will bring life and character to your aquarium. With a beautiful deep blue hue, especially evident in males as they age, this species is ideal for shrimp aquariums! Easy to care for and peaceful, these gobies eat mainly biofilm and algae, making them the perfect companions for small rasboras, tetras, loaches, and other small fish. Get your Cobalt Blue Dwarf Goby today!

What are they like?

Cobalt Blue Dwarf Goby is a peaceful, small fish that is perfect for any home aquarium. They are generally very friendly and peaceful, making them a great addition to any tank. They are also safe to keep with shrimp and other small aquatic creatures, so they make an ideal choice for those who want to keep a variety of species in their tank.

For whom is the Blue Cobalt Blue Dwarf Goby suited?

The Blue Cobalt Blue Dwarf Goby is a great beginner fish for those looking to start a community tank. It is an easy-to-care-for fish that can provide both beauty and interest to the tank. Its unique colors and behavior make it stand out from other fish, making it a great choice for those just starting out in the hobby. Not only is it easy to care for, but its peaceful nature makes it suitable for most community tanks.

The ideal conditions for the Blue Cobalt Blue Dwarf Goby

The Cobalt Blue Dwarf Goby is a unique species of fish that is found in nature inhabiting the fastest-flowing stream and living above waterfalls. They require oxygen rich water to survive. They prefer river rocks in their aquascape to simulate their natural habitat, although this is not require. In addition, they are sensitive to fluctuations in water parameters, so it is important to maintain a seasoned and stable tank for their survival. All these conditions make it important for aquarists to create an ideal habitat for the Cobalt Blue Dwarf Goby in order to ensure their survival and health.

Please Note: All fish shipments have a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee when shipping with Overnight (1 Day) option. All other options void our Live Arrival Guarantee for fish. 

What we feed our Gobies

The Cobalt Blue Dwarf Goby is an algae and biofilm grazer, and so these foods should be readily available to ensure their long-term health and happiness. A Goby enjoys plant based foods. We supplement their diet with a mix of fresh vegetables and Northfin Veggie, Community, Bug Pro, and Northfin Kelp Wafers to ensure a balanced and nutritious diet. We also feed our Gobys prepared algae wafers and let them graze on natural algae and bio-film. This can be supplemented by using Bacter A.E. which can be found by clicking here.

Our Tank Parameters

  • Ph: 5.5 to 7
  • Gh: 8 to 16
  • Kh: 4 to 8
  • TDS: 180 to 250
  • Temp: 70 to 74

products.title: Shrimply GH & KH Bundle | Shrimple by Flip Aquatics
products.body_html:
Shrimply GH & KH | Aquarium Water Remineralizer

Shrimply GH is meticulously designed to maintain optimal GH (General Hardness) levels in freshwater aquariums, particularly benefiting shrimp and other invertebrates. With an ideal range of 4 to 16 dGH (70 to 280 ppm), it is important to determine the needs of your aquatic pets. GH Levels directly impact vital processes like fish muscle and bone development, shrimp molting, snail shell formation and the growth of aquatic plants. Regular monitoring and supplementation will help foster the well-being of your aquatic pets while promoting thriving biological processes essential for a balanced aquarium.

Dosing: 1 Pump (2ml) per 10 gallons adds 1dGH 

For caridina shrimp needing a soft water setup (pH below 7), we find a GH of 4 to 6 dGH to be ideal. For Neocaridina shrimp, we recommend a 2:1 ratio of GH to KH to maintain a pH above 7, with the ideal GH level being 10-16 dGH.

Analysis: Magnesium (Mg) 2.9%, Calcium (Ca) 9.3%


Specifically formulated for freshwater aquariums, Shrimply KH (Carbonate Hardness) is crucial for stabilizing pH levels in aquariums where a pH above 7 is ideal. Without adequate KH, pH can fluctuate quickly causing stress on the animals and premature molting in shrimp. Maintaining a KH between 4 to 8 dKH (70 to 140ppm) is ideal for most freshwater aquariums. Regular monitoring and supplementation will help maintain stability, promoting a consistent environment for your aquatic pets.

Dosing:1 Pump (2ml) per 10 gallons adds 1dKH 

For neocaridina shrimp, we recommend a 2:1 ratio of GH to KH to maintain a pH above 7, with the ideal KH level being between 4 to 8 dKH. 

Shrimply KH should not be used if the ideal pH for your aquatic pets is below 7.

Each bottle is 500ml (16.09 fl oz) - You will receive 1 bottle of each Shrimply GH and Shrimply KH.

products.title: 3 Prambanan Nerite Snails
products.body_html:
Prambanan Nerite Snails

These Nerite snails offer more than just ornamental beauty—they're essential partners in aquarium maintenance. With their voracious appetite for algae, Prambanan Nerite Snails help keep your tank clean and algae-free, ensuring a healthy environment for your aquatic inhabitants to thrive. Plus, their gentle grazing habits won't harm live plants or disturb delicate substrates, making them ideal additions to any freshwater or saltwater aquarium setup.

Unlock the secret to effortless aquarium maintenance with Prambanan Nerite Snails. Their natural ability to scour surfaces for algae ensures that your tank remains pristine with minimal effort on your part. Watch as these tiny yet mighty creatures glide gracefully across your aquarium, leaving behind a trail of cleanliness and tranquility. Elevate your aquatic oasis with the beauty and functionality of Prambanan Nerite Snails, and experience the joy of a thriving and harmonious underwater world.

Background and Key Benefits

Nerite snails or Neritina sp. are freshwater snails related to the mollusk family. They will clean algae from glass, substrate, and décor. They are also so popular in our hobby because they do not reproduce in freshwater. In the wild these snails return to brackish water to breed and the baby snails require brackish water for early stages of growth. Therefore, Nerite snails will not overpopulate in an aquarium.

Along with serving practical purposes in the aquarium, the snails have  beautiful shells and they are fun to watch. If you look at your glass closely after adding them, you will see the tiny track marks these snails make as they graze on algae!

We keep these snails on pool filtered sand and in bare bottom tanks with Matten filters. We also use LED lighting to provide enough natural algae growth for them to feed. 

Please note: During shipping, snails sometimes go into a hibernation-like state and may not become fully active for up to a week once acclimated to your aquarium. 

 

Tank Parameters

  • pH: 7.2 to 8.5
  • GH: 7 to 15
  • KH:  2 to 8
  • TDS: 120 to 400
  • Temp: 68 to 78

products.title: All in One Aquarium Plant Fertilizer | Shrimple
products.body_html:
All in One Aquatic Plant Fertilizer | Shrimple

Specifically formulated for freshwater aquariums, our all-in-one fertilizer is the perfect solution for thriving planted aquariums. Utilizing our all-in-one fertilizer is imperative in delivering crucial nutrients such as nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium to sustain the optimal growth and vitality of your aquatic plants. Our beginner-friendly formula ensures optimal plant growth, while the convenient dispenser allows for effortless dosing. The bottle holds 500ml (16.09oz) of liquid and will treat up to 2,500 gallons.

Please note: In aquarium setups containing invertebrates, we recommend using our Shrimply Fertilizer due to its copper-free composition and trace quantities of nitrates (NO3). Acknowledging the susceptibility of shrimp to copper and the potential toxicity of NO3.

Directions: We suggest dosing 1-2 times per week depending on the needs of your aquarium.

1 Pump (2ml) per 10 gallons adds 5ppm NO3, 0.5ppm PO4, 5ppm K and .3ppm Fe.

If you notice algae outbreaks cut dose frequency to once a week and wait for algae to disappear then gradually work your way back up. 

Analysis: Nitrogen (N) 2.1%, Available Phosphate (P2O5) .7%, Soluble Potash (K2O) 11.5%, Iron (Fe) .58%, Magnesium (Mg) .38%, Boron (B) .02%, Copper (Cu) .0007%, Manganese (Mn) .024%, Molybdenum (Mo) .001%, Zinc (Zn) .009%

products.title: Shrimply Fertilizer for Sensitive Livestock | Shrimple by Flip Aquatics
products.body_html:
Aquarium Plant Fertilizer | Copper Free Formula

Shrimply Fertilizer is meticulously crafted to be the solution for planted aquariums housing shrimp and other delicate invertebrates. Unlike conventional all-in-one fertilizers, Shrimply Fertilizer is copper free with trace amounts of nitrates (NO3), recognizing the sensitivity of shrimp to copper and NO3 toxicity. By omitting copper and reducing NO3 levels, we ensure that both your invertebrates and your plants can thrive without the risk of serious ailments. Our beginner-friendly formula ensures optimal plant growth, while the convenient dispenser allows for effortless dosing. The bottle holds 500ml (16.09oz) of liquid and will treat up to 2,500 gallons.

Disclaimer: Trace amounts of copper are essential for many aquatic species. These trace amounts can be obtained through regular feeding of aquatic food. Nitrates are essential for plan growth and are present in balanced aquariums. NO3 levels should ideally remain at or below 20ppm for optimal invertebrate health.

Directions: We suggest dosing 2 times per week for most shrimp focused tanks.

1 Pump (2ml) per 10 gallons adds 1ppm NO3, 0.1ppm PO4, 3ppm K and .25ppm Fe.

If you notice algae outbreaks cut dose frequency to once a week and wait for algae to disappear then gradually work your way back up. 

Analysis: Nitrogen (N) .4%, Available Phosphate (P2O5) .15%, Soluble Potash (K2O) 6.8%, Iron (Fe) .47%, Magnesium (Mg) .31%, Boron (B) .02%, Manganese (Mn) .032%, Molybdenum (Mo) .001%, Zinc (Zn) .009%

products.title: Multi Cichlid | Neolamprologus Multifasciatus
products.body_html:
Multi Cichlids

The Neolamprologus Multifasciatus, often affectionately referred to as "Multies" in the aquarist community, are among the world's smallest cichlids. These captivating shell-dwelling creatures typically reach just 1-2 inches in length, making them ideal for even the coziest of aquariums. Don't let their small size fool you, though – they boast outsized charm and personality!

Multies are native to the rocky shores of Lake Tanganyika in Africa, which is renowned for its incredible biodiversity. Their striking coloration and distinctive stripes add a splash of vibrancy to any aquarium. With variations that include shades of yellow, black, and white, you can curate your own dazzling mini-rainbow beneath the surface.

Multies are known for their remarkable social behavior. They form tight-knit communities and create intricate burrow systems in the sandy substrate, turning your aquarium into a bustling neighborhood. Their communal behavior is a delight to observe, making them a fascinating addition to any tank.

These little marvels are incredibly hardy and low-maintenance. They adapt well to various water conditions, and their modest size means they don't demand much space. A 20 gallon or larger tank with a temperature range of 74-82°F and slightly alkaline water conditions is ideal, but Multi's are forgiving and can thrive in a range of setups. Make sure you have sand and plenty of shells for these shell-dwellers to call home!

They have an interesting community dynamic, so it's best to start with a group of 4 or more. Multies can also coexist peacefully with other small, non-aggressive fish species that share their water preferences.

products.title: Double Sword Guppy | Poecilia Reticulatus
products.body_html:
Double Sword

The Double Swordtail Guppy is a livebearer fish known for its sword-like tail fins, which create amazing movement in the water. This vibrant guppy comes in various colors, making it a standout in any freshwater aquarium. This species adds movement and color to community tanks.

This Swordtail Guppy is peaceful and perfect for community tanks with other non-aggressive species. Swordtail Guppies are omnivores and can be fed a balanced diet of flakes, pellets, and occasional live or frozen foods. They are an excellent choice for both beginners and experienced hobbyists.

Please Note: All fish shipments have a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee when shipping with
Overnight (1 Day) option. All other options void our Live Arrival Guarantee for fish. 

Frequently Asked Questions:

1. What do Double Swordtail Guppies eat?

We feed our fish a mix of frozen, live, and prepared foods including live baby brine and black worms. We also supplement their diet with a mix of Northfin Veggie, Community, and Bug Pro. 

2. What is the ideal tank size for Double Swordtail Guppies?

Double Swordtail Guppies are small fish and can thrive in tanks as small as 10 gallons. However, a larger tank (20 gallons or more) is recommended if you plan to keep multiple guppies or other species.

3. How many Double Swordtail Guppies should I keep together?

It's best to keep at least 3-4 females for every male in your tank to prevent aggression. Guppies are social fish and enjoy the company of their own kind.

4. Are Double Swordtail Guppies good for community tanks?

Yes, Double Swordtail Guppies are peaceful and get along well with other non-aggressive fish. They are an excellent choice for community aquariums with similar-sized species.

5. Do Double Swordtail Guppies breed easily?

Yes, Double Swordtail Guppies are livebearers and breed easily in home aquariums. Female guppies can give birth to several fry every 3-4 weeks, so it's important to have a plan for managing or rehoming the young fish.

6. Can Double Swordtail Guppies live with shrimp?

Yes, Double Swordtail Guppies can live with shrimp, as long as the shrimp are not small enough to be eaten by the guppies. Larger shrimp species like Amano or Cherry Shrimp are good tank mates.

7. Are Double Swordtail Guppies hardy fish?

Yes, Double Swordtail Guppies are relatively hardy and can adapt to a wide range of water conditions. However, like all fish, they will thrive best with regular tank maintenance and a stable environment.

products.title: Blue Glass Guppy | Poecilia Reticulatus
products.body_html:
Glass Guppy

The Glass Guppy, also known as the Blue Glassy Guppy, is a beautiful freshwater fish with a blue body and translucent scales that reflect light. Glass Guppies are peaceful by nature and thrive in community aquariums alongside other non-aggressive fish species. They are also active swimmers, adding life and movement to the aquarium without causing disruption. Their ability to coexist well with tank mates and their low-maintenance care requirements make them an excellent choice for anyone looking to enhance their freshwater setup.

Our Tank Parameters:

  • Ph: 7.2-8.2
  • Gh: 8-12
  • Kh: 4-6
  • TDS: 150-200
  • Temp: 72-80
Please Note: All fish shipments have a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee when shipping with
Overnight (1 Day) option. All other options void our Live Arrival Guarantee for fish. 

What we feed Guppies

We feed our fish a mix of frozen, live, and prepared foods including live baby brine and black worms. We also supplement their diet with a mix of Northfin Veggie, Community, and Bug Pro. 

Frequently Asked Questions:

1. Are Glass Guppies good for beginners?
Yes, Glass Guppies are an excellent choice for beginners! They are hardy, low-maintenance fish that adapt well to a variety of tank setups. Regular water changes and a balanced diet will keep them healthy and thriving.

2. What do Glass Guppy fish eat?
Glass Guppies are omnivores and enjoy a varied diet. They will readily accept:

  • High-quality flake or pellet food as a staple
  • Frozen or live foods such as brine shrimp, daphnia, and bloodworms
  • Blanched vegetables like spinach or zucchini as occasional treats

Providing a balanced diet enhances their health, color, and activity.

3. Can Glass Guppies live with other fish?
Yes, Glass Guppies are peaceful and make great community fish. They get along well with other non-aggressive species, such as:

  • Tetras
  • Corydoras catfish
  • Mollies
  • Shrimp and snails

Avoid housing them with large or aggressive fish that may nip at their fins.

4. Does the Glass Guppy breed easily?
Yes, Glass Guppies are livebearers and are known for their prolific breeding. Females can give birth to 20-50 fry every 4-6 weeks. If you want to control breeding, keep only males or separate females into a different tank. For fry survival, provide plenty of plants or hiding spots.

5. How big do Glass Guppy fish get?
Glass Guppies are small fish, typically growing to about 1.5 to 2 inches (3.8-5 cm) in length.

6. How many Glass Guppies should I keep together?
Glass Guppies are social fish and do best in groups of at least 5-6 individuals. A larger group helps reduce stress and encourages natural behavior.

7. Do Glass Guppy fish need live plants in their tank?
While live plants are not required, they provide several benefits, such as oxygenation, hiding spots, and improving water quality. Easy plants like Java Moss, Anubias, and Hornwort pair well with guppies.

8. What size tank do I need for Glass Guppies?
A 10-gallon tank is the minimum recommended size for a small group of 5-6 Glass Guppies. Larger tanks are always better, as they provide more swimming space and allow for easier maintenance.

products.title: Kohaku Guppy | Poecilia Reticulatus
products.body_html:
Kohaku Guppy

The Kohaku Guppy is a striking fish known for its red and white coloring, similar to the traditional koi fish from Japan. It has long fins that flow smoothly behind it as it swims, adding movement and interest to your aquarium. The Kohaku Guppy is a peaceful fish that does well in a variety of tank setups, making it a good choice for both beginners and experienced aquarists.

The Kohaku Guppy is easy to care for and can thrive in community tanks or in a tank dedicated to guppies. Its calm nature and beautiful colors make it a great addition to any aquarium. Whether you want to add a pop of color or just enjoy watching its graceful swimming, the Kohaku Guppy will become a favorite in your tank.

  • Ph: 7.2-8.2
  • Gh: 8-12
  • Kh: 4-6
  • TDS: 150-200
  • Temp: 72-80
Please Note: All fish shipments have a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee when shipping with
Overnight (1 Day) option. All other options void our Live Arrival Guarantee for fish. 

What we feed Guppies

We feed our fish a mix of frozen, live, and prepared foods including live baby brine and black worms. We also supplement their diet with a mix of Northfin Veggie, Community, and Bug Pro. 

Frequently Asked Questions:

What size tank does the Kohaku Guppy need?

Kohaku Guppies are small fish, and they do well in tanks that are at least 10 gallons. A larger tank provides more space for swimming and can help maintain stable water conditions. If you have a group of guppies, a larger tank will also prevent overcrowding.

Can Kohaku Guppies live with other fish?

Yes, Kohaku Guppies are peaceful and get along well with most community tank fish, such as tetras, mollies, and platies. However, avoid keeping them with very large or aggressive fish that might stress them out. They are best kept with other calm fish that are similar in size.

How do I care for Kohaku Guppies?

Kohaku Guppies are relatively easy to care for. They need a well-maintained tank with stable water conditions, regular feeding (such as high-quality flake or pellet food), and occasional water changes. They also benefit from some plant cover in the tank, as it provides hiding places and helps reduce stress.

How do I breed Kohaku Guppies?

Kohaku Guppies are livebearers, meaning they give birth to live young instead of laying eggs. To breed them, you'll need both a male and a female guppy. Female guppies can give birth to several batches of fry from a single mating. If you're not ready for baby guppies, it's important to separate the males and females to avoid overpopulation.

How can I tell the difference between male and female Kohaku Guppies?

Male Kohaku Guppies are typically smaller and more colorful, with the striking red and white pattern that makes them stand out. They also have a small, modified fin called a gonopodium used for mating. Female Kohaku Guppies are larger, less colorful, and have a rounder body shape due to carrying eggs.

What do Kohaku Guppies eat?

Kohaku Guppies are omnivores and will eat a variety of foods, including high-quality flakes, pellets, and live or frozen foods like brine shrimp and daphnia. It's important to provide a balanced diet to keep them healthy and vibrant. Feed them small amounts a few times a day, ensuring they don't overeat.

These stripes give it an eye-catching look that stands out in both small and community tanks.

This species is easy to care for, making it a great choice for beginner and experienced aquarists alike. The Tiger Guppy is active and peaceful, thriving in a variety of tank setups. It grows to about 1.5 to 2 inches in length, which makes it suitable for both small and medium-sized tanks. These guppies are best kept in groups, where they can show off their unique coloring and playful behavior.

  • Ph: 7.2-8.2
  • Gh: 8-12
  • Kh: 4-6
  • TDS: 150-200
  • Temp: 72-80
Please Note: All fish shipments have a 100% Live Arrival Guarantee when shipping with
Overnight (1 Day) option. All other options void our Live Arrival Guarantee for fish. 

What we feed Guppies

We feed our fish a mix of frozen, live, and prepared foods including live baby brine and black worms. We also supplement their diet with a mix of Northfin Veggie, Community, and Bug Pro. 

Frequently Asked Questions:

What size tank do Tiger Guppies need?

Tiger Guppies are small fish, usually growing up to 2 inches (5 cm). A 10-gallon tank is typically sufficient for a small group of guppies, but larger tanks are always better for maintaining water quality and giving them space to swim. They thrive in tanks with plants, caves, and swimming areas.

What do Tiger Guppies eat?

Tiger Guppies are omnivores, so they can eat a variety of foods. A diet of high-quality flake food or pellets works well. They also enjoy occasional live or frozen foods such as brine shrimp, daphnia, and bloodworms. A varied diet helps maintain their health and vibrant colors.

Can Tiger Guppies live with other fish?

Yes, Tiger Guppies are peaceful fish and make excellent additions to community tanks. They do well with other small, non-aggressive species like tetras, corydoras, and peaceful rasboras. Avoid keeping them with larger, more aggressive fish that might harass them.

Do Tiger Guppies breed easily?

Yes, like other guppies, Tiger Guppies are livebearers and breed easily in the right conditions. Female guppies can give birth to dozens of fry every few weeks. If you're not looking to breed, consider keeping only males or using a breeding trap to control the population.

How do I care for baby Tiger Guppies?

Baby guppies, or fry, are very small and should be kept in a separate tank or a breeding box to protect them from adult fish. They can be fed finely crushed flakes or baby brine shrimp until they grow large enough to join the main tank.

products.title: Painted Fire Red Shrimp
products.body_html:
Painted Fire Red Shrimp

Elevate your freshwater aquarium with our Painted Fire Red Shrimp (Neocaridina davidi var. "Fire Red"), known as the high-grade cherry shrimp variant. These mesmerizing shrimp captivate with their fire red coloration. Although these freshwater shrimp are stunning they're also hardy in nature and relatively easy to care for, these shrimp are perfect for both beginners and seasoned aquarists. With their adaptable disposition, they thrive in a variety of tank setups, effortlessly enhancing any tank whether their fully planted tanks or community setups. Not just stunning, Painted Fire Red Shrimp also serve a practical purpose as they're great algae eaters helping to keep your tank clean and balanced. Breeding readily in suitable conditions, they offer a fascinating glimpse into the natural world of aquatic reproduction. These shrimp are one of the highest grades with a step up above Fire Red Shrimp. Below you can see the grading scale for this variety of shrimp.

Grading Cherry Shrimp

The grading of cherry shrimp is as follows:

Cherry shrimp in general refers to the lowest grade of Red Neocaridina aquarium shrimp. Regular cherry shrimp are generally pretty clear.

Sakura cherry shrimp are a more intense red color. While still possessing clear patches, Sakura shrimp are more solid in color than regular cherry shrimp.

Fire red shrimp is the next grade and refers to shrimp that are completely red. Fire Red shrimp are the second to highest grade. 

Painted fire red shrimp are completely solid in color and have no transparent patches. Painted fire red shrimp usually have red legs as well.

products.title: 3 Batik Nerite Snails
products.body_html:
Pack of 3 Batik Nerite Snails

The batik nerite snail has eye-catching black and gold patterns that make it a perfect choice for nano and planted aquariums. Native to Indonesia, the batik nerite snail can live for more than four years, which is longer than many other snails. While adult batik nerite snails thrive in freshwater, their babies need brackish or saltwater to grow, so they won’t breed in a regular freshwater tank.

Background and Key Benefits

Nerite snails or Neritina sp. are freshwater snails related to the mollusk family. They will clean algae from glass, substrate, and décor. They are also so popular in our hobby because they do not reproduce in freshwater. In the wild these snails return to brackish water to breed and the baby snails require brackish water for early stages of growth. Therefore, Nerite snails will not overpopulate in an aquarium.

Along with serving practical purposes in the aquarium, the snails have  beautiful shells and they are fun to watch. If you look at your glass closely after adding them, you will see the tiny track marks these snails make as they graze on algae!

We keep these snails on pool filtered sand and in bare bottom tanks with a Matten filters. We feed Shrimp King Algae Pops and Shrimp King Snail Stixx along with Indian Almond Leaves for natural biofilm. We also use LED lighting to provide enough natural algae growth  for them to feed. 

Please note: During shipping, snails sometimes go into a hibernation-like state and may not become fully active for up to a week once acclimated to your aquarium. 

Tank Parameters

  • pH: 7.2 to 8.5
  • GH: 7 to 15
  • KH:  2 to 8
  • TDS: 120 to 400
  • Temp: 68 to 78

products.title: Endler's Livebearer | Poecilia wingei
products.body_html:
Endler's Livebearer | Wild Type

Endler's Livebearers (Poecilia wingei) are one of the most popular freshwater aquarium fish due to their brilliant colors and peaceful community nature. Males exhibit a kaleidoscope of colors including green, blue, red, yellow, and black in various patterns across their bodies and flowing fins. Females are less flamboyant but still attractive with yellowish-green coloration. Both sexes only reach around 1 inch in length when mature, making them perfect for nano aquariums.

Endler's are extremely prolific livebearers and breed readily in the aquarium with little intervention required. This makes them a great choice for those looking to breed their own fish and experience the miracle of live birth. They are peaceful community fish that do best when kept in schools of 6 or more. Provide them with a well-planted aquarium of at least 10 gallons. With proper care, Endler's Livebearers can live 2-3 years, bringing a constant burst of color to any community tank.

  • Temp: 72 to 80

products.title: Bumblebee Goby | Brachygobius doriae
products.body_html:
Freshwater Bumblebee Goby | Brachygobius doriae

The True Freshwater Bumblebee Goby is a small, colorful fish with black and yellow stripes, resembling a bumblebee. Unlike other bumblebee gobies that need salty water, this species lives happily in freshwater aquariums. They are perfect for small tanks and bring a lot of personality. 

Size and Appearance

These gobies grow to about 1 inch long. Their bright stripes make them stand out in any tank. Males often have brighter colors, especially during breeding times.

Behavior and Tank Setup

Bumblebee gobies are curious and like to explore. They enjoy hiding in small caves and among plants. Each fish may claim its own little spot to rest. They prefer calm water without strong currents.

Feeding

These fish are carnivores and prefer live or frozen foods like bloodworms and brine shrimp. They usually don't eat dry fish food. 

Tank Mates

Bumblebee gobies can be territorial, especially with their own kind. It's best to keep them in groups of six or more to reduce aggression. Avoid housing them with long-finned or slow-moving fish, as they may nip at them.

Water Conditions

  • Temperature: 72° - 84°F (22° - 28.9°C)

  • pH: 7.0 - 8.5

  • KH: 6 - 20

  • Minimum Tank Size: 10 gallons
